title: CNC Milling Machinist - 2nd Shift
description_text:
Who We Are
Founded in 1959 by Ed Miller near Chicago, Prince Industries has grown from a one-man shop into a global manufacturing partner. With seven facilities across the U.S. and China, we serve industries from hydraulics to aerospace, handling everything from just-in-time components to high-volume assemblies.
At Prince, we don’t just make parts—we solve complex supply chain challenges. We are a strategic partner, focused on helping clients improve products, reduce costs, and simplify operations. Certified to ISO 9001:2015, ISO 13485, and AS9100 standards, we combine quality, innovation, and growth-minded thinking to deliver real value.
JOB SUMMARY
Prince Industries is looking for a CNC Machinist in our Swiss Department.
Second Shift: Flexible shift: ~2:30 PM - 1:00AM. MUST be willing to train on the first shift. Opportunities for Overtime. 
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Operates production machinery to print specifications.
Performs a variety of tasks by using established guidelines with limited judgement to perform functions of the job.
Visually and dimensionally inspect production parts for any defects and reports defects to immediate supervisor.
Verify conformance of workpiece to specifications, using measuring instruments such as micrometers, calipers, and go-no-go gauges.
Required to use statistical process control techniques.
Relies on instructions and preestablished guidelines to perform the functions of the job.
Reads blueprints for such information as dimensions and tolerances (i.e. GD&T)
Reads and interprets the Job Traveler for such information as number of workpieces to be machined, job rate, sequence of operation, etc..
Understands and follows Prince Industries quality certifications (i.e. ISO, QS) as relates to position.
Independently perform all job duties of machine operation with little or no instruction required such as: 
Machine startup
Adjust tooling to maintain proper sizes
Replace broken or worn tooling
Identify non-functioning safety equipment or unsafe conditions
Conduct daily preventative maintenance checks to include proper coolant levels, filters, fan operation, cleans the machine as needed, etc.
Secures all tooling and instruments required (Doc: PQ01F03).
Completes necessary job related paperwork to include tags, inspection documents, production log, etc...
Cleans all spills and noticeable grease spots on floor in shop.
Responsible for maintaining the quality, protection and storage of the completed product (ex: layer packing, rust preventative oils, box weight, etc.)
Identify necessary tooling required for safe and efficient daily operation. (i.e. insert changes, drills, work holding, etc..)
Proficient in the use of basic inspection equipment (including height gauge and optical comparators)
Able to begin simple machine set ups and/or machine adjustments as needed (ex: remove and/or set tools or diamonds, reset jaws, collets, guides, etc.)
Transports finished materials, manually or using pallet jack, to area indicated on the Job Traveler.
Maintains established production rate as indicated on the Job Traveler and reports any discrepancies immediately to department supervisor.
Observes and listens to machine operation to detect malfunctions, such as worn or damaged cutting tools, etc.
Positions and secures the workpiece in holding device, machine table, chuck, centers, or fixtures.
Maintains good housekeeping in work area.
Works under direct supervision.
Understands and observes all necessary safety procedures: safety glasses, shoes, ear protection, machine interlocks, lifting guidelines, etc. as designated in specific work area assigned.
Additional responsibilities as assigned by supervisor; may include department transfers to meet production needs.
QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
Ability to recognize and interpret documents such as safety rules, operating and maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. 
Ability to communicate effectively with supervisor and co-workers as needed.
Ability to comprehend and follow simple oral or written instructions.
Ability to perform intermediate shop math skills and calculations: fraction conversions, divide, multiply, percentages, etc.
Basic computer skills required in order to perform inquiry, data collection or input.
Familiar with standard concepts, practices and procedures within production field.
EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE
High school diploma or GED equivalent
Previous experience, apprenticeship or formal training in CNC Machining
1-3 years of related CNC machining operation experience in the field required
Ability to read, write, and speak English fluently. 
PHYSICAL JOB REQUIREMENTS
Regularly required to stand and reach the duration of shift, 8-10 hours.
Frequently required to talk or hear.
Frequently required to perform repetitive or short-cycle work.
Occasionally required to walk; sit, stoop, kneel, crouch, or bend.
Regularly lift, push or pull up to 15 pounds and occasionally up to 50 pounds.
Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision in which 20/20 or corrected vision to 20/20 vision is required.
Regularly exposed to moving mechanical parts
Occasionally exposed to fumes or airborne particles resulting from metalworking coolants and oils.
Frequently exposed to warm/hot parts and machine sparks.
Frequently required to move warm/hot parts.
Frequently exposed to compressed and cryogenic gases.
The noise level in the work environment is generally loud, requiring hearing protection.
Must be able to communicate clearly and accurately in noisy environments. 
Must also have close, distance, and peripheral vision, with the ability to adjust focus and judge depth. Color vision is required as well. 
Must be able to read, write, and speak English. 
Blue Cross Blue Shield Medical insurance (PPO and HDHP with HSA) w/ Prescription Coverage
Dental PPO Insurance
Vision PPO Insurance
Short-Term & Long-Term Disability Insurance
Company Paid Life Insurance and AD & D – Including options to add on additional life insurance for yourself, spouse & children.
Critical Illness Insurance
Accident Insurance
401(k) Fidelity Plan with strong employer match
Personal Retirement Advisor
Generous PTO Policy and 9 Paid Holidays per year
100% Tuition Reimbursement for Job-Related Classes
Personal Protective Equipment reimbursement (safety glasses, shoes, etc.)
Uniform Rental Service
Employee Events
COMPENSATION: $23 - $28 Per Hour 
-Additional compensation for shift differential 
Due to ITAR regulations, only U.S. persons—defined as U.S. citizens, lawful permanent residents (Green Card holders), and U.S. nationals—are eligible for this position.
*We are an Equal Employment Opportunity ("EEO") Employer. Applicants can learn more about Prince's status as an equal opportunity employer by viewing the federal "EEO is the Law" poster ( https://www.eeoc.gov/employers )

title: Legal Intake Virtual Assistant (VA) - Phone & Call Handling Focus
description_text:
Valgorithm is a consulting firm specializing in AI-driven solutions, automation, and fractional services that help businesses streamline operations and scale efficiently. We partner with clients in the healthcare, and legal industry to optimize workflows, enhance compliance, and drive strategic growth.
We are seeking a professional, friendly, and highly communicative Legal Intake Virtual Assistant to join the Valgorithm team. In this role, you will support one of our U.S.-based legal clients primarily by answering inbound phone calls, speaking with potential clients about employment-related concerns, and completing structured, script-based intake forms using MyCase. This is a call-focused role, and the majority of your time will be spent handling phone conversations with empathy, clarity, and professionalism.
As a Valgorithm VA, you’ll be part of our internal team. You will report directly to both the client and Valgorithm. While you will serve a specific client, you are fully supported, managed, and paid through us — and we expect you to actively contribute to our culture of professionalism, ownership, and teamwork.
Responsabilities
Client Intake & Call Handling
Answer inbound phone calls with professionalism, warmth, and empathy 
Follow a structured intake form in MyCase to gather information accurately 
Ask standardized questions related to employment matters and document responses clearly 
Reassure callers and guide conversations without providing legal advice 
Escalate urgent or sensitive cases according to established guidelines 
Create a positive first impression and build trust with potential clients 
Communication & Documentation
Record accurate notes and update intake records in MyCase 
Respond to basic inquiries using provided scripts and guidelines 
Maintain clear, timely communication with the client and internal team 
Additional Support (Occasional)
Assist with light administrative tasks as needed (e.g., updating records, scheduling, or follow-ups) 
Valgorithm Team Responsibilities
Participate in internal check-ins, updates, and feedback sessions 
Follow Valgorithm’s processes, standards, and communication expectations 
Use Google Workspace, Slack, and task tools to stay organized and efficient
1+ years of experience as a legal assistant, paralegal, or virtual assistant supporting legal professionals 
Experience in a client-facing, phone-based role, such as legal intake, call handling, or customer support 
Familiarity with legal case management or CRM systems (MyCase preferred; training provided) 
Strong proficiency with Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Docs, Sheets, Drive) 
Comfortable using Slack for internal communication and coordination 
Excellent spoken and written English, with a calm, professional phone presence 
Strong communication skills, especially when handling sensitive or emotional conversations 
High emotional intelligence and empathy, with the ability to make callers feel heard and supported 
Trust-building mindset and confidence in guiding structured conversations 
Strong attention to detail and ability to follow structured processes independently in a remote environment 
Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive legal information with discretion
Work Environment Requirements
Dedicated computer (not shared) with at least 8GB RAM and up-to-date security software 
Reliable primary internet connection (minimum 20 Mbps download / 10 Mbps upload) 
Backup internet connection (e.g., mobile hotspot, pocket WiFi, or prepaid modem) 
Quiet, distraction-free workspace suitable for handling confidential phone calls 
Licensed operating system: Windows 10 or higher, or macOS v10.15.5 or higher
30-60-90 Day Plan
First 30 Days
Complete onboarding and training on tools, intake scripts, and workflows 
Shadow calls and begin handling intake calls with support 
Days 31–60
Independently handle inbound intake calls 
Accurately document information in MyCase and escalate cases as needed 
Days 61–90
Consistently manage intake calls with professionalism and empathy 
Support light administrative tasks and maintain clear communication 
Full-time, long-term role with a stable schedule 
Employed, managed, and supported directly by Valgorithm 
Structured onboarding and training with clear scripts and processes 
Paid non-working U.S. holidays (as applicable) 
Opportunity to grow within Valgorithm as the team and client base expand 
Supportive, people-centered remote work environment
ai_core_responsibilities: The Legal Intake Virtual Assistant will primarily handle inbound phone calls, speaking with potential clients about employment-related concerns and completing structured intake forms. The role requires maintaining professionalism and empathy while guiding conversations and documenting responses accurately.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least 1 year of experience as a legal assistant, paralegal, or virtual assistant in a client-facing role. Strong communication skills, proficiency with Google Workspace, and the ability to handle sensitive conversations are essential.

title: Concierge Experience Representative - Marquis MD
description_text:
Practice Healthcare the Way It Should Be
Marquis MD is a concierge medical practice built around relationships, not rushed appointments that meet quotas. Our Medical Assistants are not task-runners or room-turners — they are trusted partners in delivering calm, personalized, high-touch care to patients who expect excellence and humanity in equal measure.
If you take pride in anticipating needs, creating ease for others, and being part of a team that treats both patients and colleagues with respect, this role was designed for you.
Position Overview
The Concierge Experience Representative is the welcoming presence and central connector of the Marquis MD experience. This role ensures every patient interaction is seamless, personal, and aligned with the brand’s commitment to luxury-level care and whole-person wellness.
Patient Experience and Hospitality
Welcome and assist patients and guests in a warm, professional manner that reflects the Marquis MD standard.
Anticipate patient needs and provide an effortless, personalized experience.
Maintain an organized and elegant reception environment.
Ensure all interactions are handled with discretion and empathy.
Coordinate with the care team to support smooth patient flow.
Scheduling and Coordination
Manage appointment scheduling and confirmations.
Assist with membership inquiries, renewals, and onboarding.
Coordinate referrals and follow-ups as needed.
Ensure communication between patients and providers is clear and timely.
Administrative and Operational Support
Handle phone, email, and in-person inquiries with professionalism.
Maintain organized and accurate documentation.
Assist with forms, billing coordination, and correspondence.
Identify opportunities to enhance front-of-house efficiency.
Qualifications
Three or more years of experience in concierge medicine, private healthcare, or luxury hospitality.
Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills.
Exceptional organizational ability and attention to detail.
Polished presentation consistent with the Marquis MD brand.
Composed and professional under pressure.
Key Attributes
Warm, intuitive, and approachable.
Proactive and detail-oriented.
Dependable and team-focused.
Embodies the Marquis MD values of accessibility, excellence, empathy, and integrity.
21 paid days off (15 days of PTO, which increases with tenure, plus 6 holidays).
Flexible Spending Account (FSA) and Health Savings Account (HSA) options.
Medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and life insurance.
Generous 401(k) with up to 6% employer match.
At Marquis MD, we believe leadership, innovation, and purpose go hand in hand. Be part of a team where your work drives lasting impact and your career reaches new heights.
Marquis MD participates in the U.S. Department of Homeland Security E-Verify program.
MarquisMDConcierge
ai_core_responsibilities: The Concierge Experience Representative acts as the welcoming presence, ensuring every patient interaction is seamless, personal, and aligns with the brand's commitment to luxury-level care and whole-person wellness. This includes managing patient experience, hospitality, scheduling, coordination, and essential administrative and operational support.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess three or more years of experience in concierge medicine, private healthcare, or luxury hospitality, coupled with outstanding interpersonal and communication skills. The role demands exceptional organizational ability, attention to detail, and a polished presentation consistent with the Marquis MD brand.

title: Customer Experience Specialist
description_text:
Join a global leader in sporting goods and outdoor performance. Amer Sports is home to iconic brands like Wilson, Salomon, and Arc’teryx, known for their innovation, quality, and connection to world-class athletes and passionate lifestyle consumers. With brand headquarters in Chicago (Wilson), Annecy (Salomon), and Vancouver (Arc’teryx), our ANZ team operates from a vibrant new facility in Moorabbin, Melbourne. We’re a dynamic group of professionals committed to delivering exceptional consumer experiences—and we’re growing!
We’re currently seeking a Customer Experience Specialist to join our team. This is a fantastic opportunity to work with premium brands and gain hands-on experience in digital commerce, customer engagement, and VIP guest management.
About the Role
As a key member of the Customer Experience team, you’ll help customers fall in love with our brands by delivering outstanding service across digital channels. With your focus being on Arcteryx and Salomon, you’ll collaborate with our eCommerce team to improve the online shopping experience and high touch relationship management.
Key Responsibilities
Order & Return Management: Ensure smooth processing of orders and returns by following established protocols.
Warranty Claims: Assess and process customer warranty requests with care and accuracy.
Customer Support: Respond to inquiries via live chat, email, social media, and phone.
Product Expertise: Become a go-to advisor on our products, helping customers find the perfect fit for their needs.
Performance Goals: Contribute to achieving monthly KPIs and team targets.
VIP Guest Experience (Golden Guests)
You’ll support the day-to-day delivery of our Golden Guest (VIP) experience, helping ensure every interaction feels personal, thoughtful and on-brand. Working closely with the CRM & Loyalty Manager, you’ll help execute communications, events and touchpoints that bring the program to life. Responsibilities include:
Executing Golden Guest communications, including onboarding emails, quarterly touchpoints, event invitations and responding to inbound enquiries, following established plans and brand guidelines.
Drafting written communications in our brand tone, with guidance and review provided as you grow in the role.
Supporting the delivery of Golden Guest events, including preparation, guest coordination and on-site assistance.
Maintaining Golden Guest records, ensuring communication logs, guest details, purchase history and event attendance are accurately tracked.
Capturing guest feedback and observations and sharing insights with internal teams.
Supporting the ongoing evolution of the Golden Guest program, contributing ideas and improvements as confidence and experience build.
What You Bring
Natural people skills: you genuinely love connecting with people and bring warmth, confidence and energy to every customer interaction — from inbox to in-person VIP events.
Passion for Sports & Outdoors: You’re enthusiastic about our products and the active lifestyle they support.
Curiosity & Drive: You’re eager to learn, stay updated on tools and trends, and understand how brands operate.
Customer Insight: You understand consumer behaviour, media habits, and shopping preferences.
Exceptional Communication: You’re confident and personable in both written and verbal interactions, with a warm and professional tone.
Relationship Building: You excel at creating meaningful connections with customers, especially VIPs, and understand the importance of personalized service.
Discretion & Professionalism: You handle sensitive customer information with care and maintain a high level of confidentiality.
Event & VIP Engagement: You’re comfortable supporting events end-to-end and confidently engaging with VIP guests in social, brand-led environments.
Team Collaboration: You build strong relationships with colleagues and partners, and contribute positively to team culture.
Tech Savvy: Comfortable with Excel and data sets; experience with SAP is a plus.
Why Join Us?
Work with globally recognized brands in a fast-paced, supportive environment.
Gain exposure to digital commerce and cross-functional collaboration.
Enjoy product discounts and other great perks.
Be part of a fun, energetic, and open-minded team.
Ready to make an impact?
Apply now by submitting your CV and cover letter. We can’t wait to hear from you!
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Customer Experience Specialist, you will manage orders and returns, warranty claims, and provide customer support across various digital channels. You will also enhance the VIP guest experience by executing communications and supporting events.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should possess strong people skills, a passion for sports and the outdoors, and a drive to learn and understand consumer behavior. Exceptional communication and relationship-building skills are essential, along with the ability to handle sensitive information professionally.

linkedin_org_description:
Arc’teryx is a Canadian company born in the Coast Mountains, where our passion for the outdoors drives us to push the boundaries of performance and durability. We design gear that not only survives extreme conditions but elevates your experience in them. With over 2,400 retail locations worldwide, our impact goes beyond gear—it’s about designing for a better future.

What truly sets Arc'teryx apart is the community behind the "Bird." We're more than a team—We're a collective of problem-solvers, makers and explorers, united by a spirit of 'Leave It Better' and a deep respect for the outdoors. From Vancouver to Tokyo, Munich to New York, our teams and culture are bound by this shared purpose. 

If you're a creative thinker, a skilled maker or a gear expert who thrives on helping others connect with the outdoors, this is where your passion can find its home. 

Welcome to Arc'teryx, where we design for the wild and for those bold enough to venture into it.

title: BCBA (Hybrid In Home)
description_text:
Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) – Hybrid Role
✨ Exclusive Sign-On Bonus: Paid out over your first 9 months
✈️ Relocating? We’re happy to discuss relocation support.
About Us
At The Treetop ABA, we are committed to making a meaningful impact in the lives of children and families. We provide high-quality Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy for children with developmental needs, with a strong focus on individualized, evidence-based care.
Our mission is to deliver person-centered therapy that helps children thrive. By partnering closely with families, we ensure treatment is not only clinically effective but also supportive and collaborative.
The Treetop ABA continues to expand and is recognized for its high-quality service delivery across multiple regions, with both hybrid and in-clinic programming supported by strong clinical leadership and operational infrastructure.
The Role
We are seeking a full-time, passionate BCBA to join our team in a hybrid position. This role blends meaningful in-home/community-based clinical work with remote flexibility for planning, documentation, collaboration, and supervision.
Key Responsibilities
Manage a small, intentional caseload to support high-quality, individualized care 
Develop and oversee ABA treatment plans tailored to client needs 
Conduct assessments and track progress through data-driven decision making 
Supervise and support Behavior Technicians / RBTs 
Collaborate with families and other care providers for cohesive support 
Maintain accurate and timely documentation and compliance 
Stay current with ABA research, best practices, and ethical standards
Qualifications
Master’s degree in Behavior Analysis, Psychology, or related field 
Active BCBA certification (required) 
State LBA license (required or in process) 
Experience developing and implementing ABA programs 
Strong communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills 
Experience with diverse client populations is a plus
Why Join Treetop ABA?
Competitive Pay: $85,000–$100,000 base salary 
Attractive monthly performance bonuses with clear, attainable benchmarks
Generous Sign-On Bonus: Paid out within your first 9 months 
Hybrid Flexibility: In-home sessions plus remote administrative days 
Generous PTO: Vacation, holidays, and personal days 
$1,000 CEU Stipend: Annual continuing education support 
401(k) with Match: Invest in your future with employer support 
Referral Bonuses: Rewards for bringing great people to the team 
Career Growth: Clear advancement paths into leadership 
Manageable Caseloads: Focus on quality, not just numbers 
Relocation Support: Assistance available if you are moving to Georgia
Ready to Join Us?
If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, we encourage you to apply. The application takes only a few minutes and could be the first step toward an exciting future with The Treetop ABA.
At Treetop, we are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to creating a supportive and inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive.

title: Business Development Manager - Korea
description_text:
As Business Development Manager for Korea, you’ll drive growth by expanding Keywords Studios’ presence among leading game developers. You’ll build strong relationships, identify new opportunities, and close strategic deals and manage strategic relationships for Keywords game development and art services. You’ll act as a trusted partner to clients while shaping how we grow in one of the most exciting gaming markets in the world.
Key Responsibilities
Sales Execution & Pipeline Growth
● Drive end-to-end sales from lead generation to contract closure to achieve quarterly, and annual revenue goals.
● Develop and manage a qualified sales pipeline, balancing studio capacity with deal potential and profitability.
● Create and execute account plans for key clients to increase market share and strengthen long-term partnerships for the Keywords brand.
● Represent Keywords services at trade shows and industry events to expand brand visibility and generate leads for your assigned production team.
Client Relationship Management
● Build and nurture trusted partnerships with top-tier clients, aligning Keywords’ solutions with their 3-5 year business goals.
● Collaborate closely with Account Managers/Production to ensure client retention and satisfaction across global territories.
● Lead quarterly business reviews to identify upselling and cross-sell opportunities generated and sent to the right teams within Keywords.
● Work cross-functionally with production teams to ensure quality delivery and client success.
Market Strategy & Collaboration
● Analyze market trends, competitor activity, and client feedback to inform business strategy up to the executive team.
● Partner with Marketing to support targeted campaigns for the Japan market.
Reporting & Forecasting
● Maintain accurate pipeline data and forecast in Pipedrive CRM.
● Report weekly key wins, challenges, and competitive insights with ideas to overcome any obstacles.
● Manage operational expenses within budget and contribute to financial reporting accuracy.
People Management & Strategic Leadership
● Manage, coach, and develop a team of mid-level salespeople and sales operations professionals, fostering a culture of accountability, collaboration, and high performance.
● Conduct regular performance reviews and set clear KPIs for team members.
● Identify and implement professional development opportunities to build future leadership capability within the team.
● Develop scalable and repeatable sales programs in Korea that align with global growth objectives.
● Contribute to overall company strategy and serve as a role model for collaboration, integrity, and innovation.
● Build confidence in the Keywords brand within the Korea market.
Goals
● Achieve and exceed revenue targets across all service lines assigned.
● Build and maintain a robust, high-quality sales pipeline.
● Increase client retention and increase share of revenue within existing accounts.
● Provide clear market insights and strategic input to global leadership.
● 10+ years in B2B sales, business development, or account management, ideally in
creative services, tech, and game development.
● Proven track record of meeting or exceeding revenue targets.
● Experience managing client relationships and negotiating multi-stakeholder deals.
● Understanding the video game production pipeline is a strong plus.
● Familiarity with CRM tools (Pipedrive, Salesforce) and productivity suites (Google
Workspace, MS Office).
● Fluent English: Korean language skills strongly preferred.
● Strong passion for gaming culture and the interactive entertainment industry.
● Willingness to travel up to 50% domestically and internationally.
● Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.
Here are some of the benefits our studios can offer:
Competitive salary and benefits
Collaborate with industry leaders on AAA titles
Professional growth opportunities
Flexible work arrangements
Fully Remote opportunity 
Additional perks: IT schemes, pensions, healthcare, social events, and more.
Our Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) Commitment: Keywords Studios is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for all positions without regard to race, ethnicity, religion or belief, sex, age, national origin, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws. If you require any adjustments during the process please let us know in your application. We are committed to creating a dynamic work environment that values diversity and inclusion, respect and integrity, customer focus, and innovation. We especially welcome applications from candidates of underrepresented groups in the industry.
PERSONAL DATA PROTECTION POLICY
By providing your information in this application, you understand that we will collect and process your information in accordance with our Applicant Privacy Notice. For more information, please see our Applicant Privacy Notice at https://www.keywordsstudios.com/en/applicant-privacy-notice.

linkedin_org_description:
🎮🎬 We help make video games, films, and fan favourites you’ve probably played, watched, or heard.

We work behind the scenes with game developers, publishers, and entertainment companies to bring their ideas to life and keep them running smoothly. From game development and art production to audio services, localization, game testing, community support, and marketing, we’re the partner you call when you need extra hands (or brains).

We are 13,000 people worldwide. Tech-savvy, AI-aware, and deeply invested in R&D. We’re adaptable, collaborative, and used to plugging into diverse teams and pipelines. Many of us grew up with games, animation, and film. We’re fans first, and that shows in how we work: with care, respect, accessibility, and safety in mind.

Whether it’s building innovative experiences, meeting tight deadlines, or preserving creative vision across every language and culture, our services and proprietary tools integrate seamlessly with your team at any stage of the product life cycle.

We don’t always make the headlines, but we help others get there. 

Among our clients: Tencent Games, Microsoft, Ubisoft, Supercell, Bandai Namco, Netflix, Amazon... #ImagineMore

title: Part-Time Personal Care Attendant - North Houston
description_text:
Looking for stability, flexibility, and work that truly matters?
Christian Senior Care Services is here to support your career—especially during times of change.
Personal Care Attendant (PCA)
Conroe, TX | Zip Codes 77301
Pay: $12.20 per hour
Shifts: Monday 9am-12:30pm, Tuesday - Friday 9am-11:30pm, 13.5 hrs pr wk
If recent employer changes have impacted your job, we encourage you to reach out. We are hiring now and ready to help you move forward.
Why Christian Senior Care Services?
You will make a real difference—one client, one home, one day at a time—while enjoying the flexibility and support you deserve.
We now offer affordable benefits for part-time employees.

Flexible scheduling that fits your life
Weekly pay
Employee recognition and support
PPE provided
Optional benefits include:
Medical plans
Dental coverage
Vision coverage
Short-term disability
Life and AD&D insurance
Choose the benefits that work best for you—or select them all.

Apply today and start making a difference.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Personal Care Attendant will be responsible for assisting clients with activities of daily living, preparing and cleaning up after meals, and providing ambulatory support. title: BCBA In Clinic
description_text:
Sign-On Bonus Available
Join our team and receive a generous sign-on bonus to welcome you aboard. Ready to relocate? Let's talk - we may be able to help. Monthly performance bonuses and ask about our Ramp up bonus. 
About The Treetop ABA
At The Treetop ABA, we're more than just a company - we're a community of dedicated clinicians driven by a shared purpose: to provide exceptional ABA services to children and families. With a focus on collaboration, kindness, and evidence-based care, we've built a reputation for clinical excellence and compassionate support.
We proudly offer in-clinic services in Savannah, GA in a fun, supportive, and team-focused environment - where BCBAs are valued, respected, and empowered.
Why Join Us in Savannah?
Our in-clinic BCBAs in Savannah enjoy the perfect blend of structure and flexibility. Here's what your hybrid schedule would look like:
Clinic-based position with consistent team collaboration 
Two flexible remote days per month (one during Week A and one during Week C) 
On Weeks B & D: Most of our team finishes their day by 3:30-4:00 PM 
You'll also be joining a close-knit, positive, and fun-loving clinical team that supports one another and truly enjoys what they do.
Role Overview
We're looking for a passionate and experienced Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) to join our in-clinic team in Savannah, GA. This is a full-time hybrid role with a primary focus on in-clinic services. You'll work closely with clients, families, and technicians to deliver high-quality, individualized ABA programs in a collaborative, engaging environment.
Key Responsibilities
Manage a small, focused caseload to ensure high clinical quality 
Develop and oversee individualized ABA treatment plans 
Conduct assessments and analyze data to monitor progress 
Supervise and mentor RBTs and technicians 
Collaborate with families and clinical leadership 
Maintain accurate and timely documentation 
Stay current on ABA best practices to ensure treatment effectiveness 
Ready to Join Us?
If you're a BCBA who's passionate about making a difference - and you're looking for a workplace that values flexibility, growth, and teamwork - we'd love to meet you. Applying takes just a few minutes.
Equal Opportunity Employer
The Treetop ABA is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes candidates of all backgrounds. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
Qualifications
Master's degree in ABA, Psychology, Education, or a related field 
Current BCBA certification (required) 
Current GA LBA or in process 
Experience in ABA program development and clinical supervision 
Excellent communication, leadership, and organizational skills 
Passion for working with children and making a meaningful impact 
Benefits
Competitive salary based on experience and location 
Attractive monthly performance bonuses with clear, attainable benchmarks
Flexible PTO and eight company holidays 
Full benefits including medical, dental, vision, life, and disability insurance 
401(k) with company match 
CEU and professional development support 
Opportunities for career growth and leadership advancement 
Supportive and collaborative work environment 
Relocation assistance available 
Flexible scheduling for personal needs - no need to use PTO for appointments
ai_core_responsibilities: The BCBA will manage a small caseload to ensure high clinical quality and develop individualized ABA treatment plans. They will also supervise and mentor RBTs and technicians while collaborating with families and clinical leadership.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a Master's degree in ABA, Psychology, Education, or a related field and current BCBA certification. Experience in ABA program development and clinical supervision is required, along with excellent communication and leadership skills.

title: Board Certified Behavior Analyst -BCBA Hybrid In Home
description_text:
Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) – Hybrid Role
Exclusive Sign-On Bonus: Join us and receive a generous sign-on bonus paid out in your first 6 months.
Relocating? Let’s talk about how we can support your move.
About Us
At The Treetop ABA, we are committed to making a meaningful impact. We provide high-quality Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy for children with developmental needs, always focusing on individualized, evidence-based care.
Our mission is to deliver person-centered therapy that truly helps children and families thrive. By partnering closely with parents, we ensure treatment is not only effective but also supportive.
The Role
We are seeking a full-time, passionate BCBA to join our team in a hybrid position. This role combines direct client support in-home and in the community with remote administrative flexibility.
Key Responsibilities:
Manage small caseloads for high-quality, individualized care 
Develop and oversee ABA treatment plans 
Conduct assessments and track client progress through data analysis 
Supervise and support Behavior Technicians 
Collaborate with families and other professionals for cohesive care 
Maintain accurate and timely documentation 
Stay up to date with ABA research and best practices
Qualifications
Master’s degree in Behavior Analysis, Psychology, or related field 
Active BCBA certification (required) 
State LBA license (required or in process) 
Experience developing and implementing ABA programs 
Strong communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills 
Experience with diverse client populations is a plus
Why Join The Treetop ABA?
Competitive Pay: $85,000–$100,000 base salary 
Attractive monthly performance bonuses with clear, attainable benchmarks
Generous Sign-On Bonus: Paid out within your first 9 months 
Hybrid Flexibility: In-home sessions plus remote administrative days 
Generous PTO: Vacation, holidays, and personal days 
$1,000 CEU Stipend: Annual continuing education support 
401(k) with Match: Invest in your future with employer support 
Referral Bonuses: Rewards for bringing great people to the team 
Career Growth: Clear advancement paths into leadership 
Manageable Caseloads: Focus on quality, not just numbers 
Relocation Support: Assistance available if you are moving to Georgia
Ready to Join Us?
If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, we encourage you to apply. The application takes only a few minutes and could be the first step toward an exciting future with The Treetop ABA.
At Treetop, we are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to creating a supportive and inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive.

Key duties also include supervising Behavior Technicians and collaborating with families and professionals to ensure cohesive care.

Experience in developing and implementing ABA programs, strong communication skills, and the ability to work with diverse populations are required.

title: Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialist
description_text:
Schedule: 40 hours per week
Classification: Full Time - Exempt
Location: Remote with up to 75% travel
Salary: $65,000 + Monthly Bonus Potential
Lap of Love is a national practice dedicated exclusively to in-home end-of-life care for pets. We believe all pets and their families deserve the most compassionate and supportive end-of-life experience. Our veterinarians help pets and their parents with quality of life assessments, pain and anxiety management, end-of-life consultations, and peaceful euthanasia in the comfort of their pet’s favorite place – home.
Position Description
Lap of Love Veterinary Hospice, headquartered in Lutz, FL., seeks an empathetic and self-motivated individual with excellent communication skills to join our dynamic team as a Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialist located in the Austin, TX area. Our team of Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialists engage the veterinary community and help educate them on Lap of Love’s end-of-life veterinary services for pet families: In-home euthanasia, hospice, telehospice, and pet loss support. 
The Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialist collaborates with our veterinarians spanning the nation to help educate new, underserved and expanding markets on Lap of Love’s services. The focus is to educate and gain trust within the veterinary and pet parent community by visiting clinics, attending veterinary conferences and special events, and promotion through virtual and digital channels like virtual meetings, social posts, and email outreach during off travel times. This job does require up to 75% travel as you will be representing Lap of Love in the markets we serve across the United States.
Essential Functions and Responsibilities:
Travel to veterinary conferences and veterinary clinics in markets across the United States (75% travel)
Educate veterinary practices and their team members on the benefits of recommending Lap of Love as an in-home, end-of-life care provided to pet families
Visit a minimum of 2 Lap of Love markets per month and deliver Lap of Love educational material to support the veterinary practice and pet parents
Provide new ideas, trends, and tips to continually strengthen relationships, both in person and remotely
Attend national conferences and events to promote Lap of Love to all veterinary professionals at our conference booths, speaking engagements, and special events
Report and track results from visits, events and meetings to understand effectiveness of campaign(s). 
Experience, Education and Requirements: 
At least 2 years of sales or outreach experience preferably in the veterinary industry, required 
Live within 45 minutes of a major airport, required 
Top notch oral and written communication skills with ability to tailor message to audience needs
Strong interpersonal skills and connections and ability to build new relationships in the veterinary industry.
Prioritizing, time management, organizational skills, and ability to work with limited supervision
Familiarity with Microsoft Offices 365 (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and SharePoint), G Suite (Google Drive, Gmail, Google Calendars, and Google Sheets), Customer Relationship Management systems, and ability to learn proprietary Lap of Love software
Confident, independent thinker, and self-starter
Self-driven, collaborative, and motivated to do profound work
Excellent listening and critical thinking skills
Exemplify and convey Lap of Love values - Help above all else
Strong leadership skills
This position is a remote role requiring the ability to work independently. Hours may vary and some evening and weekend availability may be necessary to complete the necessary job functions
Physical and Other Job Requirements: 
Maintain confidentiality of all patient, client, and business information
Comply with all applicable laws, regulations, policies, procedures, and guidelines
This job posting is not intended to be an exhaustive list of duties, but it aims to highlight the typical main responsibilities of the position. At the request of their supervisor, this individual may be asked to perform additional duties or take on additional responsibilities to better meet the needs of their department.
Health Insurance Plan (Medical) with 100% employer paid HSA option available
Dental and Vision Plans
Retirement Plan (Traditional 401k with 3% match and Roth 401k)
Paid Time Off
Bereavement Leave
Paid Parental Leave
Employer-funded Lifestyle Spending Account (LSA)
Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary, and AD&D)
Short Term and Long Term Disability Insurance
Training & Development
Pet Discount Plan and Pet Insurance
ai_benefits: Short Term And Long Term Disability Insurance
ai_core_responsibilities: The specialist will engage the veterinary community to educate them on Lap of Love’s end-of-life veterinary services, including in-home euthanasia and hospice care, by visiting clinics and attending conferences. This role involves extensive travel (up to 75%) to represent the company, deliver educational materials, and track campaign effectiveness.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least two years of sales or outreach experience, preferably in the veterinary industry, and must reside within 45 minutes of a major airport. Essential requirements include top-notch communication skills, strong relationship-building abilities within the veterinary industry, and proficiency with standard office software and CRM systems.
linkedin_org_slogan: Providing compassionate, in-home, end-of-life care for pets and the people who love them…because pets are family!
linkedin_org_locations: 17804 US Highway 41 North, Lutz, FL 33549, US
linkedin_org_description:
At Lap of Love, we recognize that many veterinarians today are seeking something more: a way to reconnect with the why behind their work, to form deeper bonds with families, and to practice with presence, compassion, and purpose.

We are the nation’s largest network of veterinarians solely dedicated to end-of-life care. Our services include in-home euthanasia, telehospice consultations, in-home veterinary hospice, and pet loss support—all designed to provide a peaceful, personal experience for pets and the people who love them.

Our work is centered on the human-animal bond. We honor that connection by helping families navigate one of life’s most difficult moments with empathy, expertise, and grace.

As a Lap of Love veterinarian, you’ll serve your community in one of the most meaningful ways imaginable: by helping pets pass in the comfort of home, surrounded by love. You’ll experience the gratitude of clients, the freedom of working outside a traditional clinic, and the fulfillment of practicing in a way that truly aligns with your values.

We're looking for compassionate, self-motivated veterinarians who communicate with empathy, connect with intention, and want to make a difference, one peaceful goodbye at a time.

title: Structural Revit Designer
description_text:
RMF Engineering is an innovative, top-ranked, national engineering firm that specializes in providing design and engineering services for all aspects of building and utility infrastructure construction. RMF offers a balanced benefits package that centers on a stable and professional working environment which includes above standard health and dental insurance, paid holidays, paid vacation, and 401K plan.
RMF Engineering, Inc is currently seeking a full-time Structural Revit Designer in our Baltimore, MD office. The Civil/Structural Division provides engineering services for central utility power plants, healthcare facilities, and array of utility distribution systems (including design of underground tunnels, vaults, and utility bridges). Most Universities, Hospitals, and Government sites include central utility power plants, which provide main utility services to the campus. The Civil/Structural Division specializes in structural designs for these new facilities.
3D Modeling & Drafting: Model structural systems, including foundations, framing, steel, and concrete elements, creating detailed sections and plans.
Construction Documentation: Produce high-quality structural drawings and annotation for construction, ensuring they are ready for contractor use.
Coordination & Clash Detection: Coordinate models with architectural and MEP teams to resolve interferences (clash detection) and ensure accurate, integrated designs.
Standards Adherence: Maintain Revit project files, templates, and libraries while adhering to company and industry standards.
Project Collaboration: Work closely with engineers to translate sketches, calculations, and design concepts into actionable drawings
Requirements
Associates Degree 
5+ years of experience
Experience with Microsoft Office software.
Experience with AutoCAD and Revit
A stable and professional working environment is offered with competitive salary, excellent employee benefits, which include health and dental insurance, paid holidays, paid vacation, and 401K plan. We are an equal opportunity employer and take pride in the diversity and dedication of our staff.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves modeling structural systems, including foundations, framing, steel, and concrete elements, and producing high-quality structural drawings and annotations for construction use. The designer will also coordinate models with architectural and MEP teams to resolve interferences and adhere to company standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess an Associate's Degree and have a minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in the field. Proficiency with Microsoft Office software, AutoCAD, and Revit is mandatory for this position.

A privately held company headquartered in Baltimore, Maryland, RMF has more than 285 employees in 11 U.S. offices.

title: Inbound Marketer
description_text:
Job Title: Inbound Marketer
Job Type: Full-Time | Remote
Working Schedule: US Hours
About Pavago
Pavago is a digital growth partner for fast-moving companies. We help our clients build scalable, inbound-driven marketing systems through high-quality content, SEO, conversion optimization, and distribution. We care about results, ownership, and execution—not vanity metrics.
We’re looking for an Inbound Marketer who understands that inbound is more than SEO—it’s about creating valuable, thought-provoking content and distributing it across the right channels to drive awareness, demand, and conversions.
What You’ll Be Responsible For
Content Creation & Optimization
Create high-quality, value-driven, and thought-provoking content that educates, challenges, and builds authority
Write SEO-optimized blogs, website pages, and inbound assets aligned with search intent and funnel stage
Create content optimized for both search engines and AI platforms (LLMs, AI search, featured answers, summaries)
Develop landing pages and conversion-focused copy that turns traffic into qualified leads
Inbound Marketing Execution (Beyond SEO)
Execute inbound campaigns across multiple channels, including:
SEO & content marketing
Lead magnets (guides, checklists, playbooks, resources)
Social media content that supports inbound distribution
Website and landing page experiences
Understand how content, distribution, and conversion work together in a full inbound funnel
Lead Magnets & Conversion Assets
Plan and build lead magnets that solve real user problems and capture demand
Write copy for gated assets, CTAs, thank-you pages, and follow-up flows
Collaborate with marketing and design teams to improve conversion rates
Research & Strategy
Conduct deep research on industries, competitors, and audience pain points
Identify content gaps and opportunities across the inbound funnel
Translate insights into strong content angles and inbound campaigns
Stay up to date with SEO, inbound, AI-search, and content distribution trends
Analytics & Performance
Monitor and evaluate content and inbound performance using:
Google Search Console (GSC)
Google Analytics 4 (GA4)
SEMrush, Ahrefs, AIO
Optimize content based on performance, engagement, and conversion data
Make decisions rooted in outcomes, not just traffic
What We’re Looking For
Must-Haves
Proven experience as an Inbound Marketer, Content Marketer, or Content Writer
Strong understanding of SEO, search intent, and inbound funnels
Ability to create content that drives value, not fluff
Experience with GSC, GA4, SEMrush, Ahrefs, or similar tools
Excellent written English with strong storytelling and persuasion skills
Portfolio showing results-driven inbound or content work
Understanding of how AI is changing content discovery and search
Familiarity with social media as a content distribution channel
Nice-to-Haves
Agency experience
Experience building or promoting lead magnets
Familiarity with social media as a content distribution channel
Understanding of how AI is changing content discovery and search
Day-to-Day Responsibilities
Write, edit, and optimize inbound content across multiple formats
Build and improve lead magnets and conversion assets
Collaborate with SEO, marketing, and strategy teams on inbound initiatives
Review performance data and continuously improve content and campaigns
Research new topics, angles, and opportunities for inbound growth
Manage multiple projects while meeting deadlines
Participate in team check-ins, reviews, and feedback sessions
Interview Process
Initial Screening Call – Alignment on inbound thinking, communication, and expectations
Practical Assessment – Short inbound/content-focused task
Final Interview – Deep dive into strategy, execution, and ownership mindset
#LI-AG1

title: Senior Acquisition CPI SME
locations_alt_raw: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
locations_derived: Washington, District of Columbia, United States
description_text:
USFS is an ISO 9001 management consulting firm with headquarters in the National Capital Region and offices and staff in 12 US states. USFS staff average 10+ years of professional experience, over 80 percent of our staff have bachelor’s degrees, and over half have advanced degrees in business and technology. USFS was founded in 2010 and provides financial management, acquisition management, program management, and data analytic support services to federal and state governments. US Federal Solutions, a leading management consulting firm, is seeking Acquisition Professionals to support the US Coast Guard.
Senior Acquisition CPI SME (Acquisition Continuous Process Improvement Subject Matter Expert)
Location: Remote (must be able to support Eastern Time core hours)
Schedule: Full-time | Monday–Friday 
Work Authorization: U.S. Citizen required
Security/Access: Must be able to obtain Public Trust and be eligible for CAC issuance
About the role
We’re hiring a Senior Acquisition CPI SME to support the U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) C5ISC acquisition organization. In this role, you’ll lead and execute acquisition-focused continuous process improvement (CPI) efforts that improve acquisition planning, execution, and reporting through measurable, repeatable process improvements.
This is a strong fit for someone who understands federal acquisition, can run structured improvement initiatives end-to-end, and brings hands-on process maturity experience.
What you’ll do
Lead acquisition CPI initiatives from intake through implementation and sustainment 
Gather and organize stakeholder input and facilitate prioritization of CPI opportunities (including methods like multi-voting) 
Assess effort, risk, resource needs, and expected impacts for proposed CPI initiatives; develop supporting analysis and briefings 
Develop CPI charters, work breakdown structures, schedules, and project plans; track execution and report status monthly, quarterly, and annually 
Support acquisition Integrated Project Teams (IPTs) with facilitation, coordination, and recommendations that improve efficiency and productivity 
Define and track KPIs and performance measures to demonstrate results and continuous improvement 
Create and maintain practical tools and documentation such as process guides, desk guides, flow charts, templates, and training aids 
Ensure deliverables are clear, consistent, and ready for use in Microsoft 365 (Word/Excel/PowerPoint) and PDF formats
What we’re looking for
You can turn ambiguity into a clear, workable plan 
You’re comfortable facilitating meetings, driving decisions, and keeping initiatives moving 
You focus on measurable outcomes, not “process for process’ sake” 
You communicate well with both senior leaders and working-level teams
Required qualifications
Bachelor's Degree Required
Recent experience supporting federal acquisitions (within the last 48 months) 
Strong working knowledge of federal acquisition processes and the FAR 
Process improvement experience aligned to CMMI (or equivalent) 
CMMI Practitioner certification (required) 
Ability to build and manage project plans, schedules, risks, and stakeholder communications (MS Project or similar) 
Strong writing and documentation skills with a track record of producing usable, repeatable process products 
Security and compliance requirements
U.S. Citizenship required 
Ability to obtain/maintain Public Trust 
Eligible for CAC issuance 
Ability to handle sensitive/procurement-related information appropriately and comply with DHS/USCG information protection requirements
Employer-provided paid Medical / Dental / Vision insurance.
Employer matching 401K plan.
PTO
11 Federal Holidays
Additionally voluntary benefits to include Short / Long Term Disability, Term Life, and AD&D Insurance.
ai_remote_location_derived: Washington, District of Columbia, United States

Responsibilities include managing CPI initiatives end-to-end, gathering stakeholder input, assessing risks, developing project plans, and tracking performance metrics.

A CMMI Practitioner certification is required, along with the ability to manage projects, communicate effectively, and produce usable process documentation.

title: Chauffeur de Camion-Citerne Classe 1
description_text:
Altom Transport est une entreprise familiale reconnue pour sa fiabilité et son intégrité, avec plus de 10 terminaux stratégiques couvrant les 49 États des États-Unis ainsi que l’ensemble du Canada. Nous sommes spécialisés dans le transport de liquides en vrac pour les industries de la chimie et des hydrocarbures, offrant des solutions logistiques adaptées aux besoins de nos clients.
Chez Altom Transport, nous comprenons l’importance d’un transport sécuritaire et efficace des matières liquides en vrac, tout en valorisant un esprit familial au sein de notre équipe et avec nos clients. Nos remorques-citernes en acier inoxydable et en aluminium de pointe témoignent de notre engagement envers les normes de sécurité et d’efficacité les plus élevées.
Nous recherchons actuellement des chauffeurs de camion-citerne Classe 1 (CDL A) expérimentés pour des postes locaux, régionaux et longue distance (OTR). Si vous êtes un conducteur professionnel, dédié à la sécurité et prêt à faire partie d’une entreprise familiale dynamique, nous voulons vous rencontrer !
Description du poste :
En tant que chauffeur de camion-citerne Classe 1, vous serez responsable du transport sécuritaire et efficace de matières liquides en vrac, en respectant les délais et les exigences de nos clients.
Responsabilités :
Conduire un camion-citerne pour transporter des liquides en vrac vers et depuis les sites des clients.
Effectuer les inspections avant et après chaque trajet pour assurer la sécurité et la conformité aux réglementations.
Charger et décharger les citernes à l’aide de tuyaux ou de pompes en suivant les protocoles de sécurité.
Maintenir des dossiers précis sur les livraisons, inspections et incidents.
Communiquer efficacement avec les répartiteurs et les clients pour coordonner les livraisons.
Respecter toutes les réglementations locales, provinciales et fédérales en matière de transport de matières dangereuses.
Maintenir une charge de travail stable en respectant les exigences en matière de kilométrage ou d’heures de conduite.
Être disponible pour des opérations 24/7 en cas de besoin.
Flexibilité des congés en fonction des horaires et des besoins opérationnels.
Permis de conduire Classe 1 (CDL A) avec mentions Matières Dangereuses (Hazmat) et Citernes (Tanker).
Passeport valide (États-Unis ou France) requis pour franchir la frontière.
Minimum 23 ans.
Conformité à toutes les exigences du DOT (Département des Transports des États-Unis).
Dossier de conduite propre, démontrant un engagement envers la sécurité.
Capacité à effectuer des tâches physiques liées au chargement et au déchargement des camions-citernes.
Avantages :
Régime de retraite avec contribution de l’employeur dès le premier jour.
Assurance santé complète incluant vision, dentaire et médicaments sous Blue Cross Blue Shield.
Options d’assurance vie et invalidité.
Vacances payées, congés personnels et jours fériés.
Bonus de sécurité pour les inspections routières réussies.
Formation payée, équipements de protection fournis et camions modernes.
Remboursement des bottes de travail.
Contribution de l’entreprise à un compte d’épargne santé (HSA) jusqu’à 2 400 $.

A clean driving record demonstrating commitment to safety and full compliance with all US DOT regulations are mandatory.

We're North America's Specialized Carrier of Petroleum & Chemical Products

Since 1948, Altom Transport has faithfully served a growing roster of Fortune 500 companies and Single-load customers alike. We’re a 3rd-generation family owned and operated company with a vested interest in the success of our clients and their partners. And in the highly competitive tanker hauling business of today, Altom Transport, Inc. prides itself on an unparalleled record of success.

Throughout our history, Altom has always aspired to provide the highest level of service on every single delivery, hence our mantra “Superior Service, Every Single Delivery.” – no matter how large or small. This particular standard has positioned Altom as one of the nation’s most respected tanker companies in operation. From management and sales, to dispatch and drivers, our service standards promise a very personal, yet always professional approach. Whether hauling across town or across the country, customers can count on Altom’s service quality and consistency, bar-none.

title: BCBA (Hybrid In Home)
description_text:
Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) – Hybrid Role
✨ Exclusive Sign-On Bonus: Paid out over your first 9 months
✈️ Relocating? We’re happy to discuss relocation support.
About Us
At The Treetop ABA, we are committed to making a meaningful impact in the lives of children and families. We provide high-quality Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy for children with developmental needs, with a strong focus on individualized, evidence-based care.
Our mission is to deliver person-centered therapy that helps children thrive. By partnering closely with families, we ensure treatment is not only clinically effective but also supportive and collaborative.
The Treetop ABA continues to expand and is recognized for its high-quality service delivery across multiple regions, with both hybrid and in-clinic programming supported by strong clinical leadership and operational infrastructure.
The Role
We are seeking a full-time, passionate BCBA to join our team in a hybrid position. This role blends meaningful in-home/community-based clinical work with remote flexibility for planning, documentation, collaboration, and supervision.
Key Responsibilities
Manage a small, intentional caseload to support high-quality, individualized care 
Develop and oversee ABA treatment plans tailored to client needs 
Conduct assessments and track progress through data-driven decision making 
Supervise and support Behavior Technicians / RBTs 
Collaborate with families and other care providers for cohesive support 
Maintain accurate and timely documentation and compliance 
Stay current with ABA research, best practices, and ethical standards
Qualifications
Master’s degree in Behavior Analysis, Psychology, or related field 
Active BCBA certification (required) 
State LBA license (required or in process) 
Experience developing and implementing ABA programs 
Strong communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills 
Experience with diverse client populations is a plus
Why Join Treetop ABA?
Competitive Pay: $85,000–$100,000 base salary 
Attractive monthly performance bonuses with clear, attainable benchmarks
Generous Sign-On Bonus: Paid out within your first 9 months 
Hybrid Flexibility: In-home sessions plus remote administrative days 
Generous PTO: Vacation, holidays, and personal days 
$1,000 CEU Stipend: Annual continuing education support 
401(k) with Match: Invest in your future with employer support 
Referral Bonuses: Rewards for bringing great people to the team 
Career Growth: Clear advancement paths into leadership 
Manageable Caseloads: Focus on quality, not just numbers 
Relocation Support: Assistance available if you are moving to Georgia
Ready to Join Us?
If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, we encourage you to apply. The application takes only a few minutes and could be the first step toward an exciting future with The Treetop ABA.
At Treetop, we are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to creating a supportive and inclusive workplace where every employee can thrive.
ai_core_responsibilities: The BCBA will manage a small caseload, developing and overseeing individualized ABA treatment plans based on client needs and progress tracking. Key duties also involve supervising Behavior Technicians and collaborating closely with families and other providers.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a Master's degree in Behavior Analysis, Psychology, or a related field, along with active BCBA certification and a state LBA license (or be in process). Strong skills in communication, collaboration, problem-solving, and experience implementing ABA programs are essential.

title: Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialist
description_text:
Schedule: 40 hours per week
Classification: Full Time - Exempt
Location: Remote with up to 75% travel
Salary: $65,000 + Monthly Bonus Potential
Lap of Love is a national practice dedicated exclusively to in-home end-of-life care for pets. We believe all pets and their families deserve the most compassionate and supportive end-of-life experience. Our veterinarians help pets and their parents with quality of life assessments, pain and anxiety management, end-of-life consultations, and peaceful euthanasia in the comfort of their pet’s favorite place – home.
Position Description
Lap of Love Veterinary Hospice, headquartered in Lutz, FL., seeks an empathetic and self-motivated individual with excellent communication skills to join our dynamic team as a Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialist located in the Richmond or Fredericksburg, VA areas. Our team of Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialists engage the veterinary community and help educate them on Lap of Love’s end-of-life veterinary services for pet families: In-home euthanasia, hospice, telehospice, and pet loss support. 
The Veterinary Clinic Relationship Specialist collaborates with our veterinarians spanning the nation to help educate new, underserved and expanding markets on Lap of Love’s services. The focus is to educate and gain trust within the veterinary and pet parent community by visiting clinics, attending veterinary conferences and special events, and promotion through virtual and digital channels like virtual meetings, social posts, and email outreach during off travel times. This job does require up to 75% travel as you will be representing Lap of Love in the markets we serve across the United States.
Essential Functions and Responsibilities:
Travel to veterinary conferences and veterinary clinics in markets across the United States (75% travel)
Educate veterinary practices and their team members on the benefits of recommending Lap of Love as an in-home, end-of-life care provided to pet families
Visit a minimum of 2 Lap of Love markets per month and deliver Lap of Love educational material to support the veterinary practice and pet parents
Provide new ideas, trends, and tips to continually strengthen relationships, both in person and remotely
Attend national conferences and events to promote Lap of Love to all veterinary professionals at our conference booths, speaking engagements, and special events
Report and track results from visits, events and meetings to understand effectiveness of campaign(s). 
Experience, Education and Requirements: 
At least 2 years of sales or outreach experience preferably in the veterinary industry, required 
Live within 45 minutes of a major airport, required 
Top notch oral and written communication skills with ability to tailor message to audience needs
Strong interpersonal skills and connections and ability to build new relationships in the veterinary industry.
Prioritizing, time management, organizational skills, and ability to work with limited supervision
Familiarity with Microsoft Offices 365 (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and SharePoint), G Suite (Google Drive, Gmail, Google Calendars, and Google Sheets), Customer Relationship Management systems, and ability to learn proprietary Lap of Love software
Confident, independent thinker, and self-starter
Self-driven, collaborative, and motivated to do profound work
Excellent listening and critical thinking skills
Exemplify and convey Lap of Love values - Help above all else
Strong leadership skills
This position is a remote role requiring the ability to work independently. Hours may vary and some evening and weekend availability may be necessary to complete the necessary job functions
Physical and Other Job Requirements: 
Maintain confidentiality of all patient, client, and business information
Comply with all applicable laws, regulations, policies, procedures, and guidelines
This job posting is not intended to be an exhaustive list of duties, but it aims to highlight the typical main responsibilities of the position. At the request of their supervisor, this individual may be asked to perform additional duties or take on additional responsibilities to better meet the needs of their department.
Health Insurance Plan (Medical) with 100% employer paid HSA option available
Dental and Vision Plans
Retirement Plan (Traditional 401k with 3% match and Roth 401k)
Paid Time Off
Bereavement Leave
Paid Parental Leave
Employer-funded Lifestyle Spending Account (LSA)
Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary, and AD&D)
Short Term and Long Term Disability Insurance
Training & Development
Pet Discount Plan and Pet Insurance
ai_core_responsibilities: The specialist will engage the veterinary community to educate them on Lap of Love’s in-home end-of-life veterinary services through clinic visits, conference attendance, and digital outreach. Essential functions include traveling up to 75% of the time to educate practices, delivering educational materials, and reporting on campaign effectiveness.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least two years of sales or outreach experience, preferably in the veterinary industry, and must reside within 45 minutes of a major airport. Strong oral and written communication, excellent interpersonal skills for building industry connections, and proficiency with Microsoft Office/G Suite/CRM systems are required.
linkedin_org_slogan: Providing compassionate, in-home, end-of-life care for pets and the people who love them…because pets are family!
linkedin_org_locations: 17804 US Highway 41 North, Lutz, FL 33549, US
linkedin_org_description:
At Lap of Love, we recognize that many veterinarians today are seeking something more: a way to reconnect with the why behind their work, to form deeper bonds with families, and to practice with presence, compassion, and purpose.

We are the nation’s largest network of veterinarians solely dedicated to end-of-life care. Our services include in-home euthanasia, telehospice consultations, in-home veterinary hospice, and pet loss support—all designed to provide a peaceful, personal experience for pets and the people who love them.

Our work is centered on the human-animal bond. We honor that connection by helping families navigate one of life’s most difficult moments with empathy, expertise, and grace.

As a Lap of Love veterinarian, you’ll serve your community in one of the most meaningful ways imaginable: by helping pets pass in the comfort of home, surrounded by love. You’ll experience the gratitude of clients, the freedom of working outside a traditional clinic, and the fulfillment of practicing in a way that truly aligns with your values.

We're looking for compassionate, self-motivated veterinarians who communicate with empathy, connect with intention, and want to make a difference, one peaceful goodbye at a time.

title: Plumbing Journeyman - Manufacturing Facility
description_text:
Mark III Construction is seeking a Carded Journeyman Plumber to work in our Manufacturing Facility here in Sacramento. Plumbers will perform tasks associated with the assembly and installation of pipes, fixtures, and other plumbing used for water distribution and wastewater disposal.
Mission: Leading the Evolution of Construction
Vision: Exposing the World to a New Way to Build
Core Values:
Teamwork
Integrity
Excellence
Continuous Improvement
Perseverance
Five (5) years of commercial/industrial plumbing experience or a combination of field experience and education in the trade. 
Must be able to read and understand construction documents and schedules
Understands the entire scope of the job
Able to manage portions of projects and be the “right hand” to the Foreman 
Capable of forecasting tasks and materials
Must be knowledgeable of relevant codes and regulations
Capable of doing basic take offs
Able to prepare equipment and tool lists
Experience working on large complex projects
Certification in the operation of equipment and/or in other pertinent areas is a plus
Ability to take initiative and be self sufficient 
Safety focused at all times
Possess leadership and mentorship skills
Must be able to pass a pre-employment drug screen
Must have a valid driver’s license, a clean driving record, and reliable transportation
Able to collaborate with other trades and have the “Power of One” mindset 
Brand Ambassador for Mark III Construction

Physical Demands of posted job: The physical demands described are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.
Required to lift objects weighing up to 25lbs
Required walking for long and short distances, walking from one worksite to another.
Required to perform repetitive motion tasks
Required to pull or push using force

title: Account Executive
description_text:
Job Title: Account ExecutivePosition Type: Full-Time, RemoteWorking Hours: U.S. Business Hours
About the Role
We are looking for a Account Executive to own the entire sales process end to end — from first touch to close and clean handoff. This is not an SDR or appointment-setting role. You will be responsible for closing new clients by running structured, consultative sales conversations with small business owners.
This role operates in a highly structured, process-driven, and tech-forward environment. Onboarding is bootcamp-style and self-paced, with defined modules, scripts, courses, and assessments. Success in this role requires emotional resilience, comfort with complexity, and the ability to learn independently without shutting down when things feel overwhelming early on.
ResponsibilitiesFull-Cycle Sales Ownership
Own the complete sales cycle: inbound + outbound → pre-qualification → booked calls → discovery → proposal → close → clean handoff 
Run structured 45-minute discovery calls and 10-minute pricing previews 
Close 3+ new clients per week by executing a repeatable, disciplined sales process 
Present proposals the same day and confidently move deals to close 
Consultative Selling to SMB Owners
Speak directly with overwhelmed small business owners 
Guide conversations calmly and clearly without adding confusion 
Ask strong discovery questions to uncover real business pain 
Discuss pricing confidently without hesitation or reliance on discounting 
Pipeline Management & Follow-Up
Maintain strict pipeline hygiene: every lead has a next step and follow-up date 
Execute multi-touch follow-up sequences independently 
Close deals without relying on others to chase, rescue, or intervene 
Systems, Tools & Learning
Work across approximately 20 different systems and tools 
Complete structured onboarding through self-paced learning modules and quizzes 
Use internal knowledge bases and AI-powered resources to solve problems independently 
Adapt quickly in a process-heavy, organized, and AI-forward environment 
What Makes You a Perfect Fit
You are emotionally resilient and can handle feeling overwhelmed early without disengaging 
You thrive in structured environments with clear processes and expectations 
You take ownership of numbers, outcomes, and execution 
You are confident, steady, and clear when guiding SMB owners through decisions 
You prefer consultative selling over pressure or price-based tactics 
Required Experience & Skills
Proven full-cycle closing experience (lead to closed-won); not SDR-only or appointment setting 
Experience selling to small and medium-sized business owners 
Comfortable running structured calls and closing without hesitation 
Strong follow-up discipline and pipeline management mindset 
Experience selling recurring monthly offers (subscriptions or retainers) 
Ability to clearly explain past performance metrics (quota, close rate, pipeline) 
Experience in speed-to-lead, high-volume sales environments 
High level of independence and self-service problem-solving 
Preferred (Nice to Have)
Experience selling accounting, bookkeeping, payroll, HR, fintech, SaaS, or merchant services to SMBs 
Familiarity with basic business finance concepts (cash flow, P&L, bookkeeping challenges) 
Background in payroll (ADP, Paychex, Gusto), PEO, HR/benefits, payments, lending, or SMB SaaS sales 
Key Metrics for Success (KPIs)
3+ new clients closed per week 
Strong close rates without discount-driven tactics 
Clean pipeline with accurate next steps and follow-ups 
Consistent progression through each sales stage 
Clean handoff with expectations correctly set 
Interview Process
Initial Screening 
Sales Interview 
Final Interview 
Offer & Onboarding
#LI-AG1
ai_core_responsibilities: The Account Executive will own the entire sales process from first touch to close, including running structured discovery calls and closing new clients. They will also manage the sales pipeline and follow up with leads independently.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have proven full-cycle closing experience and be comfortable selling to small and medium-sized business owners. Strong follow-up discipline and the ability to manage a sales pipeline are essential.
