title: Director of Product (Remote)
description_text:
Visit.org is looking for a passionate and ambitious Director of Product to join our remote team. The Director of Product will maximize efficiency in a constantly evolving environment, where processes are fluid and creative solutions are the norm. The right candidate will have an entrepreneurial spirit, extreme passion for our mission, and the energy and discipline to come in and be a builder in a start-up tech environment. 
What Motivates Us
There is room for everyone! We empower each other to innovate and create processes that use technology to generate meaningful, lasting change. We enjoy the challenge of finding solutions to problems that are not a way around it but through it. We’re driven by the opportunity to help enterprises and their employees around the world to contribute to their communities. 
When You Join the Team
You’ll join a movement from the ground floor and a team of purpose-driven people with a strong sense of responsibility, ownership, and pride that we’re building this thing together. 
You’ll combine passion, purpose, and a paycheck—Visit.org team members get out of bed every day knowing their work is meaningful and has a tangible impact on individuals and communities around the world.


About Visit.org:
Visit.org helps companies discover & book thousands of carefully curated social impact team experiences, led by and benefitting local nonprofits. With its proprietary library of team-based experiences across 90+ countries, Visit.org provides HR, CSR, and Meetings & Events leaders highly scalable, culturally appropriate content for purpose-driven employee and client engagement. Some of our customers include Colgate, Paramount, Visa, HubSpot, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, and more.
Responsibilities:
Own Visit.org's overall product strategy, collaborate with the CTO and COO to ensure the effective and timely launch and execution of products. 
Drive product development, create a long-term strategy and vision, and prioritize work to ensure prompt and high-quality delivery of the roadmap.
Lead the ideation, technical development, and launch of innovative global-scale enterprise products.
Lead an AI-first product strategy by embedding AI capabilities into the product roadmap and driving adoption of AI tools to optimize internal product development processes.
Create a collaborative and dynamic team culture. Proactively plan the structure, scale and develop the product team. 
Set processes for your team, working closely with business partners to ensure all are working towards the same goals.
Become an expert with our software, customers, and market, and be able to identify, prioritize, and execute on tactical and strategic projects
Work cross-functionally with other teams, such as sales, account management, and marketing to make sure their objectives are met, opinions are considered, and plans are communicated.
Define metrics that inform the success of our products.
Collect and analyze feedback from customers, stakeholders, and other teams to shape requirements, features, and end products
Manage Data Strategy to support strategic goals
Direct the collection, processing, and interpretation of data from product usage, sales pipelines, and market research.
Present insights from market research to stakeholders in monthly strategy meetings.
Conduct periodic design reviews of major projects with C-level stakeholders. 
Passion for our mission and the desire to make an impact in the world through technology
8+ years of product management experience, with at least 4 years in a team leadership role
4+ years of experience product management in B2B SaaS, with proven success delivering for enterprise or upmarket segments.
Proven experience defining and delivering AI-enabled products, along with hands-on use of AI tools to enhance decision-making, operations, and cross-functional workflows.
History of delivering results and leading with a balanced focus on both analytical
Strong data skills in quantitative analytics and qualitative user research
Exceptional at taking ideas and ambiguous data, defining the right outcome, and then mapping the right strategy and roadmap to get there
Passion for world-class UX and a sharp eye for detail.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to bring stakeholders on board.
Self-starter mindset, thriving in dynamic, fast-moving environments.
What we offer:
This is a remote, full-time role with a competitive salary and health benefits. 
How we care
Health, Dental, Vision
Unlimited PTO + Holiday + Birthday off! 
Unlimited Social Impact Time Off (SITO)!
A company-wide annual paid holiday break between Christmas and New Year’s Eve to rest and recharge
Competitive salary
Mission-aligned company events/volunteering
Inclusive, exciting start-up culture
Accelerated career & personal growth
Culture Club and more!
Salary range $150,000 to $180,000 DOE; however, base pay offered may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. A range of benefits may include equity, healthcare benefits, and paid time off may be provided as part of the compensation package.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Director of Product will own the overall product strategy, collaborating with executive leadership to ensure timely product launches and execution. Key duties include driving product development, creating a long-term vision, prioritizing work for roadmap delivery, and leading the launch of innovative global enterprise products.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess 8+ years of product management experience, including at least 4 years in a leadership role, with 4+ years in B2B SaaS delivering for enterprise segments. Proven experience defining and delivering AI-enabled products and strong data skills for analytics and user research are essential.

title: Data Administrator Analyst
description_text:
POSITION GOAL: 
Creates, publishes, and monitors Engineering reports through PowerBi, ensuring that it is modeled by a common data structure that satisfies the functional requirements of the area, and supports overall business objectives.
Supports the optimization of business systems internal to Engineering and as affected, cross functionally through the process of continuous improvement.
ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: 
Creates, maintains and publishes Engineering PowerBi business reports.
Develops, maintains and improves Engineering SharePoint sites and Visual Studio programs. Responsible for the training of affected personnel.
Participates in and supports Engineering process improvement projects, system upgrades and modifications, to ensure continuity of data integrity, and optimal use of system functionality, 
Analyze and identify departmental business system processes for improved automation and reporting.
Incorporate automation into Engineering business systems data collection for reporting purposes
Creates and maintains process documents pertaining to the creation and maintenance of PowerBi reports, SharePoint sites, Visual Studio programs, etc.
Degree / diploma in a related business systems discipline
2-3 years’ experience in data analysis/report development. 
Experience in the design/development of SharePoint sites, PowerBi reports, Visual Studio, Visual Basic, Power Query
Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize work
Detail oriented
Ability to work independently
Strong teamwork and communication skills
Travel Requirement: Less than 5% of the time.
OUR WHY: We exist to move people. Our mission is to design, deliver, and support market-leading bus and motor coach solutions that are safe, accessible, efficient, and reliable.
NFI Group | We Move People - YouTube
Leveraging 450 years of combined experience, NFI is leading the electrification of mass mobility around the world. With zero-emission buses and coaches, infrastructure, and technology, NFI meets today’s urban demands for scalable smart mobility solutions. Together, NFI is enabling more livable cities through connected, clean, and sustainable transportation. News and information is available at www.nfigroup.com, www.newflyer.com, www.mcicoach.com, nfi.parts, www.alexander-dennis.com, arbocsv.com, and carfaircomposites.com.
WHY JOIN US:
Be a part of a team leading the world’s electrification of mass mobility
Competitive Wages and comprehensive benefit package with Immediate benefit eligibility 
Paid holidays and vacation
Passionate about creating a better product, a better workplace, and a better world 
Inclusive workplace culture that values and empowers team members.
On-the-job training in a continuous learning environment (we’ve invested 10.9 million in 2023)
Advancement opportunities within our family of companies
New Flyer is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity, ensure equitable experiences and are committed to creating an inclusive environment reflective of the communities we operate. Accommodations are available at all stages of the recruitment process, at the candidates’ request.
ai_core_responsibilities: The primary goal is to create, publish, and monitor Engineering reports using PowerBi, ensuring the data structure meets functional requirements and supports business objectives. This role also involves supporting the optimization of internal Engineering business systems and cross-functional processes through continuous improvement.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a degree or diploma in a related business systems discipline and have 2-3 years of experience in data analysis or report development. Required technical experience includes designing and developing SharePoint sites, PowerBi reports, Visual Studio, Visual Basic, and Power Query.
linkedin_org_locations: Kernaghan Ave, Winnipeg, Manitoba R2C, CA
106 National Dr, Anniston, Alabama 36207, US
6200 Glenn Carlson Dr, St Cloud, Minnesota 56301, US
linkedin_org_description:
About New Flyer: New Flyer is North America's heavy-duty transit bus leader and offers the most advanced product line under the Xcelsior® and Xcelsior CHARGE™ brands. It also offers infrastructure development through New Flyer Infrastructure Solutions™, a service dedicated to providing safe, sustainable, and reliable charging and mobility solutions. New Flyer actively supports over 35,000 heavy-duty transit buses (New Flyer, NABI, and Orion) currently in service, of which 8,600 are powered by electric motors and battery propulsion and 1,900 are zero-emission. Further information is available at www.newflyer.com.

About NFI Group: Leveraging 450 years of combined experience, NFI is leading the electrification of mass mobility around the world. With zero-emission buses and coaches, infrastructure, and technology, NFI meets today’s urban demands for scalable smart mobility solutions. Together, NFI is enabling more livable cities through connected, clean, and sustainable transportation.

With 8,200 team members in nine countries, NFI is a leading global bus manufacturer of mass mobility solutions under the brands New Flyer® (heavy-duty transit buses), MCI® (motor coaches), Alexander Dennis Limited (single and double-deck buses), Plaxton (motor coaches), ARBOC® (low-floor cutaway and medium-duty buses), and NFI Parts™. NFI currently offers the widest range of sustainable drive systems available, including zero-emission electric (trolley, battery, and fuel cell), natural gas, electric hybrid, and clean diesel. In total, NFI supports its installed base of over 100,000 buses and coaches around the world. NFI common shares are traded on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol NFI. News and information is available at www.nfigroup.com, www.newflyer.com, www.mcicoach.com, www.arbocsv.com, www.alexander-dennis.com, and www.nfi.parts.

title: Full Time Hospice Case Manager
description_text:
We’re Hiring an RN – Hospice at Home with AdvisaCare
This isn’t rushed care.
This is presence, compassion, and honoring each moment.
As a Hospice RN, you’ll:
Provide one-on-one care in the comfort of home
Support patients and families with dignity and heart
Spend real time where it matters most
Care without the chaos of hospital halls
Care the way it’s meant to be—gentle, personal, and deeply meaningful.
Apply today & bring comfort home.
No On Call requirements as we have a team dedicated to after hours and weekends! 
Please Forward your resume’ so we may schedule a time to discuss your Career Journey and let’s make a difference together! We are a "Successful" and "GROWING" fast paced Hospice Agency that desires your help!!
Are you looking for a challenging and rewarding position as a Full time Hospice RN Case Manager? We are searching for compassionate, dynamic, energetic, organized and efficient professionals with Hospice experience to become part of our Hospice Family for our Bay City/Midland/Saginaw servicing the Tri City areas
For this role, you need to be a registered nurse who sees the value of Hospice care. You will be providing emotional support, with the goal to help patients live their final days with dignity and comfort. Our Hospice RN's will conduct appropriate supervisory visits which allow for clinical observation of performance of assigned team members. Participate and provide feedback to the team for regular interdisciplinary group meetings; on the development, review and revision of the patient/family plan of care.
Does this describe you? Then you may be a great fit for the Hospice RN role at AdvisaCare Hospice!!
Initial and on-going assessment of patient’s physical, functional, psychosocial and environmental needs
Implement the individualized plan of care and revisions as needed
Compliance with AdvisaCare Hospice Policies and Procedures, Medicare compliance
Consulting with and educating the patient/family on disease process, self-care techniques, end-of-life care, dealing with issues of ethical concern
Supervision of paraprofessionals providing services to patient
Preparing clinical and progress notes, participating in IDG conferences
Participate in Hospice performance improvement program
Maintain high level of customer satisfaction with our clients, families and caregivers
Communication of information using current process and technology within organization
Requirements:
Current Michigan License in good standing
Minimum 1 year hospice experience
Empathetic approach to patients and caregivers
Participate in field visits daily, weekly office meetings as indicated
Ability to adapt to patient’s status and needs on a full time on call basis
Rewards AdvisaCare Can Offer YOU!
401K Retirement Plan
Medical Benefits Available for 30+ Hourly Employees
Ability to Earn PTO
Flexible Scheduling
Excellent Pay/Weekly Paychecks
Employee Appreciation Program
Rewarding Work Environment
Paid General Orientation
High-Tech Clientele
Advanced Skilled Training Offered
24/7 Staffing Support
ai_core_responsibilities: The Hospice RN provides one-on-one care, offering emotional support and dignity to patients in their final days, while also conducting supervisory visits for team members and participating in interdisciplinary group meetings for care plan revision. Responsibilities include initial and ongoing assessment of patient needs, implementing the plan of care, ensuring compliance with policies, and educating families on disease process and end-of-life care.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be a registered nurse with a current Michigan License in good standing and a minimum of one year of hospice experience, demonstrating an empathetic approach to patients and caregivers. The role requires the ability to adapt to patient status needs and participate in daily field visits along with scheduled office meetings.
linkedin_org_locations: 4234 CASCADE RD SE, Grand Rapids, Michigan 49546, us

title: Gérant, Alliances stratégiques Marketing / Manager, Strat. Alliances Mktg (Cont)
description_text:
Veuillez noter que ce poste est un contrat temporaire destiné à remplacer une employée en congé de maternité
Aperçu du poste :
Le responsable du marketing des alliances stratégiques aura pour mission principale d'aider l'équipe marketing des alliances stratégiques à établir des rapports, à mettre en place des campagnes et à communiquer à l'entreprise sur les partenariats technologiques. 
Sous la direction du directeur principal du marketing des alliances stratégiques, le titulaire de ce poste est chargé de gérer les activités liées au programme de partenariat technologique, aux rapports et analyses des partenaires, ainsi qu'au parrainage des alliances stratégiques. Ses fonctions impliquent également de travailler en étroite collaboration avec d'autres services tels que les ventes, le service juridique, les achats et divers groupes de développement de l'écosystème des partenaires. 
Votre journée en un coup d'oeil : 
Gérer les tâches/responsabilités du ou des coordinateurs marketing des alliances stratégiques
Gérer la partie marketing du programme de partenariat technologique
Recueillir, suivre et analyser les résultats de performance des partenaires technologiques et fournir des informations sur les améliorations, les lacunes et les opportunités
Créer des présentations à partir des rapports destinés aux équipes commerciales / marketing / produits
Identifier les nouvelles opportunités de partenariat stratégique afin de répondre aux besoins régionaux, verticaux et des groupes d'application en matière de marketing
Collaborer avec plusieurs départements de l'écosystème des partenaires afin de garantir la responsabilité et la réalisation en temps voulu des activités d'intégration, ainsi que la promotion des partenariats en interne et en externe
Gérer la création de contenu, les campagnes de promotion conjointes et les réseaux sociaux liés aux partenaires du programme de partenariat technologique
Gérer tous les contrats de parrainage des partenaires technologiques et travailler avec l'équipe chargée des événements afin de s'assurer que tous les partenaires sont assurés de participer aux événements/salons professionnels de l'entreprise
Collaborer avec le département des créances pour créer des factures pour le programme de partenariat technologique, le package marketing et le suivi des paiements et des parrainages pour les événements 
Être responsable du suivi du processus de routage de tous les contrats d'alliance stratégique et des accords de non-divulgation pour signature 
Gérer le centre de partenariat Microsoft/portail Marketplace afin de fournir des opportunités de co-vente à l'organisation commerciale 
Gérer les agences pour les propositions de valeur conjointes avec les partenaires technologiques
Ce qui fait de vous un excellent candidat :
Leader naturel et motivateur d'équipe sur divers projets, avec une approche positive et axée sur les objectifs
Excellent communicateur, excellentes compétences en communication écrite et orale, capable d'adapter son style à divers publics internes et externes
À la fois analytique et enthousiaste, avec une approche proactive des tâches, qu'elles soient importantes ou mineures 
Bien organisé, soucieux du détail, axé sur les délais et les résultats 
Capable de travailler à la fois en équipe et de manière autonome 
Attitude analytique, minutieuse et axée sur le travail d'équipe 
Fait preuve d'une positivité authentique et d'une capacité naturelle à rassembler les gens, favorisant une collaboration et une confiance solides entre collègues
Excellentes compétences en gestion du temps et des projets 
Esprit ouvert et créatif – capable de traduire des idées et des concepts de manière visuelle 
Capable de travailler dans un service au rythme soutenu, avec des délais serrés 
Capacité à voyager jusqu'à 50 % du temps 
Capacité à travailler selon des horaires flexibles au siège social de Genetec 
Maîtrise du français et de l'anglais, à l'oral comme à l'écrit (ce poste nécessite d'interagir avec nos collaborateurs et clients internationaux) 
Votre expertise technique : 
Plus de 5 ans d'expérience pertinente dans le domaine des alliances stratégiques et du marketing des partenaires technologiques 
Licence ou master apprécié 
Maîtrise de Microsoft Office Suite, Excel et Powerpoint 
Connaissance des CRM, de la gestion des données et de PowerBI appréciée, connaissance de Wrike appréciée 
Expérience en matière de budget, d'ERP et d'achats, un atout 
Excellentes capacités de résolution de problèmes et aptitude à réfléchir et à agir rapidement dans des circonstances imprévues 
Solides compétences interpersonnelles et capacité à bien travailler avec des groupes interfonctionnels et des parties prenantes 
Solides compétences relationnelles 
Solides compétences en matière de présentation 
Solides compétences en matière de recherche 
Expérience de travail avec des équipes marketing, commerciales et produits, un atout 
Voilà ce que nous offrons !
Régime de rémunération attrayant
Programme de remboursement des frais de formation
Équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée grâce à un horaire de travail flexible
Repas subventionnés à notre incroyable Bistro (Les Cordons Bleus)
Café et fruits gratuits à volonté
Espace de stationnement gratuit pour tous les employés
Centre de conditionnement physique sur place avec entraîneur personnel, ainsi que plusieurs ateliers de santé et de bien-être
Si vous souhaitez savoir à quoi ressemble l’environnement de travail chez Genetec, voici le lien vers notre vidéo d’entreprise: Culture à Genetec 
Nous savons que la diversité des parcours et des expériences apporte une grande valeur à nos équipes. Même si vous ne cochez pas toutes les cases nous vous encourageons à postuler – votre profil pourrait nous surprendre!
Merci pour votre candidature, mais veuillez noter que seul(e)s les candidat(e)s sélectionné(e)s seront contacté(e)s. Les chasseurs de têtes et les agences de recrutement ne sont pas autorisés à soumettre des CV par l'intermédiaire de ce site web ou directement aux gestionnaires.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Please note that this role is a temporary contract serving as a maternity leave replacement
Job overview:
The Manager, Strategic Alliances Marketing –focus will be to help support the Strategic Alliance Marketing team with reporting, building campaigns and communicating to the company about the technology partnerships. 
Under the direction of the Senior Manager, Strategic Alliances Marketing, the incumbent in this position is responsible to managing activities linked to the Technology Partner Program, Partner reporting & Analytics as well as, Strategic Alliances sponsorship. Duties would also involve working closely with other departments such as Sales, Legal, Procurement and various Partner Eco System Development Groups. 
Your day at a glance:
Manage the duties/tasks of the Strategic Alliances Marketing Coordinator(s) 
Manage the Marketing portion of the Technology Partner Program 
Gather, track, and analyse Technology Partner performance results and provide insights on improvements, gaps and opportunities 
Create presentations from the reporting for the Sales/Marketing/Product teams 
Identify the new strategic alliance partnership opportunities to address regional, vertical and application group needs for Marketing 
Collaborate across multiple Partner Eco System departments to ensure accountability and timely delivery of integration activities as well as promoting partnerships internally and externally

linkedin_org_description:
Leading technology provider of business intelligence, unified physical security, public safety, and operations. Genetec develops open-platform software, hardware, and cloud-based services for the physical security and public safety industry. Its flagship product, Security Center, unifies IP-based video surveillance, access control, and automatic license plate recognition (ALPR) into one platform. A global innovator since 1997, Genetec is headquartered in Montreal, Canada, and serves enterprise and government organizations via an integrated network of resellers, integrators, and consultants in over 159 countries. Genetec was founded on the principle of innovation and remains at the forefront of emerging technologies that unify physical security systems. 

Fournisseur chef de file de technologie pour des solutions unifiées en intelligence d’affaires, sécurité physique, sécurité publique et opérations. Genetec développe des logiciels à plate-forme ouverte, de l’équipement informatique et des services basés sur le cloud pour le secteur de la sécurité physique et de la sécurité publique. Son produit phare, Security Center, unifie la vidéosurveillance IP, le contrôle d'accès et la reconnaissance automatique des plaques d'immatriculation (ALPR) en une seule plate-forme. Innovateur mondial depuis 1997, Genetec, dont le siège social est situé à Montréal, au Canada, est au service des entreprises et des organismes gouvernementaux par l'intermédiaire d'un réseau intégré de revendeurs, d'intégrateurs et de consultants dans plus de 159 pays. Genetec a été fondé sur le principe de l'innovation et reste à la pointe des technologies émergentes qui unifient les systèmes de sécurité physique.

title: Full Time Hospice Case Manager
description_text:
We’re Hiring an RN – Hospice at Home with AdvisaCare
This isn’t rushed care.
This is presence, compassion, and honoring each moment.
As a Hospice RN, you’ll:
Provide one-on-one care in the comfort of home
Support patients and families with dignity and heart
Spend real time where it matters most
Care without the chaos of hospital halls
Care the way it’s meant to be—gentle, personal, and deeply meaningful.
Apply today & bring comfort home.
No On Call requirements as we have a team dedicated to after hours and weekends! 
Please Forward your resume’ so we may schedule a time to discuss your Career Journey and let’s make a difference together! We are a "Successful" and "GROWING" fast paced Hospice Agency that desires your help!!
Are you looking for a challenging and rewarding position as a Full time Hospice RN Case Manager? We are searching for compassionate, dynamic, energetic, organized and efficient professionals with Hospice experience to become part of our Hospice Family for our Bay City/Midland/Saginaw servicing the Tri City areas
For this role, you need to be a registered nurse who sees the value of Hospice care. You will be providing emotional support, with the goal to help patients live their final days with dignity and comfort. Our Hospice RN's will conduct appropriate supervisory visits which allow for clinical observation of performance of assigned team members. Participate and provide feedback to the team for regular interdisciplinary group meetings; on the development, review and revision of the patient/family plan of care.
Does this describe you? Then you may be a great fit for the Hospice RN role at AdvisaCare Hospice!!
Initial and on-going assessment of patient’s physical, functional, psychosocial and environmental needs
Implement the individualized plan of care and revisions as needed
Compliance with AdvisaCare Hospice Policies and Procedures, Medicare compliance
Consulting with and educating the patient/family on disease process, self-care techniques, end-of-life care, dealing with issues of ethical concern
Supervision of paraprofessionals providing services to patient
Preparing clinical and progress notes, participating in IDG conferences
Participate in Hospice performance improvement program
Maintain high level of customer satisfaction with our clients, families and caregivers
Communication of information using current process and technology within organization
Requirements:
Current Michigan License in good standing
Minimum 1 year hospice experience
Empathetic approach to patients and caregivers
Participate in field visits daily, weekly office meetings as indicated
Ability to adapt to patient’s status and needs on a full time on call basis
Rewards AdvisaCare Can Offer YOU!
401K Retirement Plan
Medical Benefits Available for 30+ Hourly Employees
Ability to Earn PTO
Flexible Scheduling
Excellent Pay/Weekly Paychecks
Employee Appreciation Program
Rewarding Work Environment
Paid General Orientation
High-Tech Clientele
Advanced Skilled Training Offered
24/7 Staffing Support

title: Technical Project Manager
description_text:
About Medical Guardian: 
Medical Guardian is a fast-growing digital health and safety company on a mission to help people live a life without limits. With 13 consecutive years on the Inc. 5000 list of Fastest Growing Companies, we’re redefining what it means to age confidently and independently. 
We support over 625,000 members nationwide with life-saving emergency response systems and remote patient monitoring solutions. Trusted by families, healthcare providers, and care managers, our work is powered by a culture of innovation, compassion, and purpose. 
Job Overview: We are seeking a Project Manager who can bridge business strategy with technology execution. This role requires strong end-to-end process flow ownership, advanced technical requirements gathering, and the ability to strategically align projects with organizational goals. The ideal candidate will be hands-on with tools and systems, able to connect technical details to business outcomes, and skilled in leading cross-functional teams through complex agile project lifecycles. 
Key Responsibilities: 
Lead projects from initiation to launch, ensuring scope, timelines, and budgets are achieved. 
Identify and track ROI of a project, highlighting and cautioning against decisions or situations that may impact the value of the overall deliverable. 
Map and optimize end-to-end business and technical process flows, identifying gaps and improvements. 
Develop comprehensive project plans that integrate dependencies across IT, Operations, Member Experience, Product, and more. 
Make informed, effective decisions when presented with multiple options to ensure project progression. 
Partner with business analysts, engineers, and architects to gather and translate technical requirements into actionable project tasks. 
Ensure system integrations are designed, documented, and tested properly. 
Oversee UAT planning and execution, ensuring scenarios simulate real-world environments. 
Act as the bridge between technical teams and executive leadership, simplifying complex concepts for non-technical audiences. 
Anticipate risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks; proactively recommend mitigation strategies. 
Align all deliverables with broader business strategy, customer experience goals, and compliance needs. 
Drive sprint planning, backlog grooming, and reporting using tools such as Jira, Confluence, Asana, and Excel. 
Promote best practices in Agile/Scrum while balancing flexibility for project-specific needs. 
Ensure clear and consistent documentation of technical workflows and decisions. 
Requirements: 
Experience: 5+ years in project management, with at least 2–3 years in a technical project manager or systems-focused role. 
Technical Skills: Strong understanding of enterprise systems (CRM, ERP, WMS, APIs, IoT platforms) and ability to interpret technical architecture diagrams and data flows. 
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, or related field. 
PMP, CSM, Agile or equivalent certification is a plus. 
Core Competencies: 
Strong requirements gathering and documentation skills. 
Ability to think strategically while executing tactically. 
Skilled in cross-functional leadership, particularly across IT, Operations, Member Experience and Product teams. 
Excellent communication skills with both technical and business stakeholders. 
Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship now or in the future. 
Performance Expectations: 
Set clear timelines and ensure that all project milestones are met on time. 
Deliver projects with minimal defects, strong adoption, and measurable business value. 
Maintain detailed process documentation that can be leveraged for future enhancements. 
Proactively identify and solve system or process inefficiencies. 
Consistently demonstrate strategic foresight while driving day-to-day execution 
Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision)
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick Time Off & Holidays)
Company Paid Short Term Disability and Life Insurance
Retirement Plan (401k) with Company Match
ai_core_responsibilities: Lead projects from initiation to launch while ensuring scope, timelines, and budgets are achieved. Act as a bridge between technical teams and executive leadership, simplifying complex concepts for non-technical audiences.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 5+ years of project management experience, with at least 2-3 years in a technical project manager role. A bachelor's degree in Business, Information Systems, or a related field is required, and certifications like PMP or CSM are a plus.

title: Technical Solution Engineer / Full time
description_text:
At Smeetz, we are dedicated to revolutionizing the way visitor attractions manage their sales and optimize their revenue. As a unified commerce Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform powered by AI, we provide attractions such as theme parks, museums, and theaters with an all-in-one solution to effectively manage, market, and price their products.

We are a fast-growing SaaS startup, and we're looking for a Technical Solution Engineer to join our dynamic team. This role is a great opportunity to contribute to a growing company, work with a tight-knit team, and help shape our customer service operations.
You will provide advanced technical support to end-users, collaborating with developers and the customer service team for efficient issue resolution.
Key Responsibilities
Assist with L3 technical support tasks, gaining a solid understanding of the product and its features.
Collaborate with the development team to help resolve more complex issues (L3) for users.
Join daily meetings with the customer service team to help address urgent issues and work together to solve problems effectively.
Support key performance goals (KPIs) and service agreements (SLAs) to help speed up bug fixes and improve the software.
Work with account managers on account setup issues and solutions for specific customer needs, using your technical skills to support these requests.
Stay informed about our product to answer customer questions accurately. Utilize feedback from customer interactions to suggest improvements for our product and enhance the overall customer experience.
Document knowledge gained to help with bug resolution and contribute to ongoing product updates.
Excellent command of English. French would be a plus.
Bachelor degree in Computer Science.
Experience with web development, data management, and debugging.
Solid understanding of technical troubleshooting, particularly in L2 and L3 support.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams, including developers.
Eagerness to learn and contribute to process automation and software improvement.
Through this position, you will have the opportunity to grow in a fast-growing scale-up
Flexible work hours and the option to work remotely.
You will work in a highly performance-driven environment.
A vibrant and inclusive work environment where your voice matters.
Your successes will be recognised and rewarded with increased earning potential and ownership responsibilities.
If you're looking to join a startup where you can make a significant impact, we'd love to hear from you. Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter detailing why you believe you're the right fit for this role.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Technical Solution Engineer will provide advanced technical support to end-users and collaborate with the development team to resolve complex issues. They will also document knowledge gained and suggest product improvements based on customer feedback.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a Bachelor degree in Computer Science and experience with web development, data management, and debugging. Excellent communication skills and a solid understanding of technical troubleshooting are also required.

By unifying commerce, embedding financial tools, and leveraging AI-driven insights for marketing, pricing, and operations, Smeetz empowers venues to increase revenue, optimize efficiency, and deliver exceptional guest experiences.

description_text:

title: Direct support Professional
description_text:
Creed Medical is eager to welcome compassionate and dedicated individuals as Direct Support Professionals (DSP) to our team. In this integral role, you will provide essential support to individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities, facilitating their daily living activities and promoting their independence. As a DSP, you will create a positive and nurturing environment, helping clients achieve personal goals and improving their overall quality of life. This position offers a chance to build meaningful relationships with those you support while actively participating in community engagement and empowerment activities. If you have a caring heart and a drive to make a difference, this role at Creed Medical could be the perfect fit for you.

Key Responsibilities
Assist individuals with personal care, daily living skills, and therapeutic activities.
Support clients in achieving their personalized care plans and goals.
Encourage community involvement and facilitate social activities.
Document progress and communicate effectively with team members regarding individual needs.
Maintain a safe and supportive environment for clients.
Adhere to all policies and procedures, ensuring compliance with regulations.
High school diploma or equivalent; relevant coursework in social services or healthcare is preferred.
Experience in direct support or related experience is a plus.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build rapport with clients and families.
Ability to work independently while being part of a collaborative team.
Valid driver's license and reliable transportation required.
Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends.
CPR and First Aid certification, or the ability to obtain within a specified timeframe.
Flexible Schedule.

title: Sales Assistant
description_text:
Share in something more with a career at COBS Bread
2024 Top 50 Best Workplaces in Canada ™ by Great Place to Work
Our Role
When you work with COBS Bread, you’re part of a workplace that is committed to providing you with an experience that will help you rise to any occasion. We’re here to encourage you to grow, connect with your community, have fun with your team, and to share in something more. We’ve got exciting plans in place for you as a passionate, inquisitive and eager team player who wants to make an impact.
Your Role as Sales Assistant
At COBS Bread, we celebrate fresh. Our Sales Assistants are experts in freshness, and in giving our customers a great in-bakery experience. Right now, we are looking for personable Sales Assistant to share their enthusiasm for all things fluffy and sweet with our loyal customers. No experience necessary- just the right attitude and a willingness to learn.
What Your Day Looks Like
Work as part of a small team, providing an exceptional customer experience, and getting to know your regular, loyal customers
Be responsible for merchandising product and executing marketing campaigns
Work alongside your team to keep the bakery clean, and assist with production as needed. (Yep- you’re going to learn how to do some baking!)
Why You Were Bread for This
You are someone who takes pride in providing a great and personable experience for our customers
You have a positive attitude and friendly demeanour
You are reliable, self-motivated, and enjoys learning new skills
You are a team player who is approachable and likes working with people
Relevant retail experience and experience in handling cash, credit card and debit transactions is a strong asset
Physical Requirements
Continuous standing/ walking for all tasks
Constant use of both hands
Frequent lifting and carrying up to 7.5 kg., reaching below shoulder level, bending and turning
Occasional lifting and carrying up to 22 kg, pushing, pulling, crouching, bending and reaching above shoulder level
Perks & What You’ll Feel Good About
A fun team work environment with flexible hours
Our training program – Continue to develop your sales and leadership skills through our Bronze and Silver Sales Development Qualification program.
Career advancement opportunities – We have a stake in helping passionate team members grow and have more impact in the business and community.
Complimentary products on us every shift
And more, including benefits for all full time employees
While we thank you for your interest, only those selected for interviews will be contacted.

About COBS Bread
With over 130 locations, COBS Bread serves fresh and delightful baked goods to communities across Canada every day. Any unsold items are donated daily to various local charities and food banks. The company's success can be attributed to the quality of our product, the development of our people, and our warm and friendly customer service.
COBS Bread is part of Bakers Delight – Established in 1980 operating as Bakers Delight in Australia and New Zealand, and since 2003 as COBS Bread in Canada and the USA. Together we have grown to more than 700 bakeries across Australia, New Zealand, Canada and the USA. The entire network employs over 15,000 people, serving more than 2.1 million customers each week.
Accessibility
Accommodation is available upon request for applicants and employees with disabilities. If you are contacted for a job opportunity, please advise us of any accommodations needed to ensure you have access to a fair and equitable process. Any information received relating to accommodation will be addressed confidentially.

title: Senior Business Data Analyst – Commercial Health Insurance
description_text:
We are seeking an experienced Business Data Analyst with strong analytic and reporting skills. This role reports to the Chief Actuary. The ideal candidate will have prior experience analyzing health insurance claims, policy, and customer data to identify trends, improve quality of care, and reduce costs. The role also involves data extraction, analysis, visualization, and presentation to support data-driven decision-making for stakeholders. Candidates should have strong experience with Power BI, Python, and SQL. While this is a remote role, you must reside in the United States and in the Eastern or Central time zone. 

Multi-tasking and a "roll up your sleeves" attitude are very much a must.
About Evry Health and Globe Life
We are on a mission to bring humanity to health insurance. Our high-technology health plans expand benefits, increase access and transparency, and feature a personalized, human approach. We strive to ensure members live happier, healthier lives.

Evry Health is the major medical division of Globe Life (NYSE:GL). Globe Life has 16.8 million policies in force, and more than 3,000 corporate employees and 15,000 agents. For more than 45 consecutive years, Globe Life has earned an A (Excellent) rating or higher from A.M. Best Company.
\n

Roles & Responsibilities
Data Extraction and Management: Pull health plan, member, provider, and operations data from various sources and databases using SQL.
Data Analysis: Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and discrepancies across different types of operating data. 
Reporting and Visualization: Create reports, dashboards, and visualizations to present complex findings in an accessible format for both technical and non-technical audiences/stakeholders.
Collaboration: Work with teams across the company to manage different types of data requests and to implement and evaluate data-driven solutions. 
Data Quality: Ensure data integrity across our systems by identifying discrepancies and recommending/implementing data capture and maintenance standards/processes. 

Experience & Skills Desired
Education: Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Health Information Management, or a related field.
Experience: 7+ years of experience in a data analysis role, preferably in the commercial health insurance industry (not Medicare, Medicaid, or individual market/ACA plans).
Advanced SQL skills required, including experience extracting and manipulating large datasets for reporting.
Advanced Power BI and Python skills also required.
Proficiency with Microsoft Excel and Microsoft Suite 
Ability to interpret complex data and make informed decisions
Strong analytical and decision-making skills
Excellent communication (written & verbal) and interpersonal skills
Bonus: Experience with implementing automation

Telecommuting Requirements
This is a remote position. Our whole company works remotely. Company headquarters are in Dallas, Texas.
Company business hours are weekdays 9-5 CST. We will only consider candidates in the United States who reside in the CST or EST time zones.
Required to have a dedicated work area established that is separate from other living areas and provides information privacy.
Ability to keep all company sensitive documents secure.
Must live in a location that receives an existing high-speed internet connection/service.

Benefits
Competitive salary
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance as well as life and disability
Retirement savings plan with company match
Generous time off/vacation
Professional development opportunities
Flexible work environment

\n
$120,000 - $150,000 a year
\n
Evry Health is an EEO employer - Read More Here

title: Mechanical Superintendent
organization: North America Construction (1993) Ltd. - NAC Constructors Ltd.
description_text:
North America Construction (1993) Ltd. – NAC Constructors Ltd. (NAC) is well known for our expertise in water and wastewater construction across Canada. Our self-performing team of almost 500 skilled professionals and experts in trades and engineering, comprehensively handles projects, from conceptualization to delivery. We also have extensive experience in fields such as civil, structural, process mechanical, building mechanical, electrical and instrumentation work. At NAC, our priority is to maintain the highest level of quality and safety. The depth of our resources is evident in the variety of projects we construct; from water filtration to wastewater treatment; dams to ethanol; we build it all….and more!

Position Summary

The Trade Superintendent is responsible for managing and supervising mechanical site construction work and is accountable each day for delivering a high standard of HSE, quality and productivity.
\n

Responsibilities
Thorough knowledge and understanding of NAC'S Health, Safety, and Environment policies and procedures as per the roles and responsibilities within the HSE Manual.
Build and maintain a good work ethic and working relationship with the site team, the entire project workforce including subcontractors, suppliers, owner and engineer; encourage positive morale.
Knowledgeable in your trade. Take necessary action to ensure the required work is fully understood.
Responsible for the complete, thorough working knowledge of the estimate and the applicable material and labour cost codes and accountable to the Project Superintendent and the Trade Operations Manager for meeting or beating the codes. 
Participate in the creation, maintenance, and execution of NAC defined work packages used throughout the projects lifecycle.
Proficient in scheduling mechanical trades, to ensure the sequencing of the tradework is optimized to achieve the best financial results.
Execute the highest level of coordination with other trades so as not to delay the overall work.
Properly plan, monitor, inspect and follow up on material and equipment deliveries so as not to impede the progress of the work.
Accountable for proper material storage, protection, and maintenance to ensure delivered product is not lost or damaged up to the point of commissioning and turn-over.
Responsible for the planning, inspection, proper use, care and control of all tools for this trade work.
Be responsible for site organization & cleanliness of work areas, materials storage and tool cribs.
Organize and control material purchases to effectively manage quantities allowing for low levels of surplus, waste, unnecessary costs due to abuse and inventory levels.
Provide draft written planning aid to the Project Superintendent by the end of each week in preparation for the weekly Planning Aid meetings.
Prepare and approve time sheets.
Understand and administer the terms of the collective agreement and NAC’s policies and procedures.
Complete and thorough knowledge of contract drawings and specification interpretation necessary to effectively purchase and carry out the work of this trade. 
Accountable for understanding, addressing and executing all assigned items on the Project Overview Report along with the Financial Opportunities and Risk Log.
Identify quality, quantity and scope changes to the contract and communicate to the Project Superintendent and Trade Operations Manager.
Assist the Project team in preparing claims for extra work as well as advising them of possible savings due to changes in the work, etc.
Ensure that all work performed is within the contract and that all additional work is not executed without authorization from the Project Superintendent.
Responsible for providing trade-specific technical expertise to the project management team for the implementation of RFIs, RFS, CRX, commissioning plans and requisitions. Ensure the focus remains in the field.
Responsible for maintaining current record drawings (as-built drawings) for this specific trade work; drawings to be updated at least monthly.
Understand and interpret NAC’s contractual obligations for the specific area of responsibility. Accountable for meeting the project-specific Quality Plan and providing a final product that meets the requirements of NAC’s contract with the Owner. 
Work closely with the Project Superintendent, Project Manager, and the applicable Trade Operations Manager to assist in the preparation, monitoring and update of schedules (both Project Schedule and Planning Aids) to ensure dates are met or improved upon.
Assist and participate in recruiting potential candidates, approval for new hires and ensuring smooth transition and training for new hires.
Accountable to ensure the workforce is given adequate, clear direction and they understand the expectations regarding safety, quality, productivity, performance, conduct, etc.
Enforce accountability for meeting production targets starting with the foreman through to the lead hand and workforce.
Encourage and promote open communication and trust with the workforce; lead by example.
Provide regular performance feedback to your workforce. Coach and Discipline when necessary.
Identify and develop internal employee(s).

Qualifications
Must possess a Mechanical Trade certification in Plumbing or Pipefitting.
Minimum of 5 years' experience managing/leading up to 20 Plumbers, Pipefitters and Millwrights, including mechanical apprentices.
Strong organizational skills.
Proficient use of MS Word, Excel and Outlook.
Knowledge of the Occupational Health, Safety Act.
Ability to lead and assess trade performance on the job.
Able to quickly identify problem situations, evaluate alternative causes and produce workable solutions.
Strong communication, problem-solving and interpersonal skills.
Results-oriented with the ability to interpret and effectively manage multiple and competing priorities and timelines.

\n
NAC Offers:
- Competitive compensation
- Opportunities for career growth, development and advancement, including financial assistance
- Comprehensive benefit plan (dental, vision, health, etc.)
- Employee & Family Assistance Program
- Excellent company matched pension plan
- Registered Retirement Savings Plans
- Additional time off between Christmas and New Year’s Day
- Community involvement opportunities
- Friendly working environment
- Numerous team activities and various company events

NAC is committed to providing equal opportunities for all individuals to achieve their goals. If you feel that you need accommodations during the recruitment and selection process because of illness/disability, please contact Human Resources for further information
ai_benefits: Additional Time Off Between Christmas And New Year’s Day
Numerous Team Activities And Various Company Events
ai_core_responsibilities: The Mechanical Superintendent is responsible for managing and supervising mechanical site construction work, ensuring high standards of health, safety, environment, quality, and productivity. They must coordinate with various trades, manage material deliveries, and maintain site organization.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a Mechanical Trade certification in Plumbing or Pipefitting and have a minimum of 5 years of experience managing a team of mechanical trades. Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills are essential.

linkedin_org_locations: 21 Queen Street, Morriston, Ontario N0B 2C0, CA
linkedin_org_description: North America Construction (1993) Ltd. / NAC Constructors Ltd. provides true Master Builder services to the municipal, civil, biofuel and energy marketplace. With a strong commitment to safety excellence and successful project execution, the company has grown far beyond expectations since its conception back in 1993. NAC employs numerous highly skilled, certified tradespeople, along with a highly experienced team of management professionals across Canada.  Together, we've successfully completed projects ranging from large-scale municipal (water & wastewater treatment), dams and heavy civil construction projects.

title: Nurse Practitioner
description_text:
Nurse Practitioner – Skilled Nursing Facility
Schedule: Full-time, On-Site, Monday–Friday

Make a Difference in the Lives of Seniors

Join Ultra Health Providers, a mission-driven organization providing high-quality primary care to residents in skilled nursing and senior living communities. As a Nurse Practitioner, you’ll deliver patient-focused care, collaborate with interdisciplinary teams, and play an essential role in improving the health and quality of life of older adults.

Compensation and Benefits:
Base Salary: $100,000 – $120,000
Bonus Opportunities
Paid Medical Malpractice Insurance
Comprehensive Health, Dental, and Vision Insurance
Health Savings Account (HSA)
401(k) with Employer Match
Paid Time Off

Company Background
Ultra Health is dedicated to improving reaction times and patient outcomes. Our unique model provides on-site medical coverage to extend the reach of doctors, hospitals, and health systems. Working together with facility, health system, and network partners, Ultra Health helps speed recovery and lower rates of hospital readmission.

Position Overview
Working collaboratively as a member of the medical staff, the APRN will play a critical role in managing the medical needs of residents within skilled nursing facilities. The APRN will build strong working relationships with administrators and staff throughout their designated building and serve as the primary point of contact for emergencies and urgent care visits. Ultra Health provides our APRNs with the training and tools to do more of what they love—working independently to see patients and treat complex medical conditions.
\n

Responsibilities
Provide primary, urgent, and preventative care for residents in assigned skilled nursing facility. May also provide care to assisted living facility residents.
Verify admission orders and conduct initial assessments of residents. Conduct physical exams, medication reviews, and depression screenings.
Collaborate with the facility medical director, attending physicians, and nursing staff to deliver the highest level of care.
Communicate with and counsel family members on treatment options when there are material changes in status.

Minimum Qualifications
Valid Nurse Practitioner License in applicable state
DEA License
Nurse Practitioner national certification
Clinical background in adult, family, or geriatric specialties
COVID-19 vaccination required unless qualified for a remote, religious, or medical exemption

Preferred Qualifications
Experience in internal medicine, gerontology, or cardiology
Excellent administrative, organizational, and communication skills
Ability to work independently
Detail-oriented, dependable, and reliable
Nursing experience in LTC/SNF a plus

\n
$100,000 - $120,000 a year
Benefits:
401(k)
401(k) matching
Dental insurance
Health Savings Account
Health insurance
Paid time off
Vision insurance
Paid Medical Malpractice Insurance



Supplemental Pay:
Bonus opportunities
\n
Make a meaningful impact in the lives of seniors. Join Ultra Health and help us improve the health and well-being of our aging population.

Apply now at ultrahealthproviders.com
ai_core_responsibilities: The Nurse Practitioner will provide primary, urgent, and preventative care for residents in skilled nursing facilities, collaborating with medical staff to ensure high-quality care. They will also manage emergencies and urgent care visits, building strong relationships with facility staff.

Experience in internal medicine, gerontology, or cardiology is preferred, along with excellent communication and organizational skills.

title: Program Manager, Architecture & Design
description_text:
Are you ready to join a growth-oriented team where creativity meets innovation? At Ware Malcomb, we are a dynamic and forward-thinking design firm committed to pushing the boundaries. Our team-oriented, collaborative approach ensures that every project is a blend of visionary design, seamless project delivery, and we are actively engaged with both the community and the industry. Discover our vibrant culture to get an inside look into life at Ware Malcomb and the programs we offer. https://waremalcomb.com/life-at-ware-malcomb/ 

As a Program Manager at Ware Malcomb, you will be responsible for developing a program's objectives and strategy to assess how it will impact the department, business, and organization. You will define and oversee the projects to reach their targeted goals and implement strategic tasks that align new programs with the organization’s business strategy and goals.

This position will focus on high levels of coordination for programs and clients which include 1 in 3 Fortune 500 companies. Types of projects include high tech manufacturing and logistics facilities, automated distribution, retail, robotics, electric vehicles, aviation, sustainable and green technologies.
\n

Your Role
Accomplish strategic objectives by overseeing multiple project activities
Plan and monitor overall program execution
Project coordination and project team management
Create and maintain program budgets
Manager resources across multiple projects
Stakeholder communications, negotiations, and problem-solving
Communicates job expectations by planning, monitoring, appraising, and reviewing job contribution
Achieves operational objectives by contributing information and recommendations to strategic plans and reviews and prepare action plans
Implement production, productivity, quality, and customer-service standards by resolving problems, completing audits, and identifying trends determining system improvements, and implementing change
Meet financial objectives by preparing an annual budget, scheduling expenditures, analyzing variances, and initiating corrective actions

Qualifications
10+ years of experience within the field of architecture, construction or real estate and a minimum of 5 years of management experience
Program Management experience including staffing, planning, and people management, managing performance and profitability, financial planning, promoting process improvement and strategic planning
Adept at dealing with complexity, analyzing information, conflict resolution and implementing company vision
Highly analytical and organized
Excellent teambuilders
Great negotiator and influencer
Must have exceptional leadership and communication skills.
Preferred equivalent of a Bachelor’s Degree in Architecture or related field

\n
This is a full-time, long-term position. 

Established in 1972, Ware Malcomb is a dynamic, forward-thinking commercial real estate design firm providing professional architecture, planning, interior design, civil engineering, branding, building measurement, structural engineering and MEP services to clients throughout the world. 

With office locations throughout the United States, Canada, Mexico and Brazil, the firm specializes in the design of office, industrial, science & technology, healthcare, multifamily, retail, and public/institutional projects.

For six consecutive years, Ware Malcomb has been ranked as the #1 Industrial Sector Architecture Firm by Building Design + Construction Magazine. The firm is also ranked among the top 10 Architecture/Engineering firms in Engineering News-Record's Top 500 Design Firms and the Top 30 Interior Design Firms in Interior Design magazine's Top 100 Giants. For more information, visit www.waremalcomb.com.

title: Warehouse Technician
description_text:
Warehouse Technician
Tucson, AZ

Company Overview

World View is a next-generation intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) provider delivering integrated, multi-domain solutions across air, stratosphere, and space. By unifying tactical UAVs, high-altitude platforms, fixed-wing aircraft, and low-Earth orbit satellites under a single operational and technological framework, the company enables seamless data fusion and real-time decision-making.
Leveraging advanced AI and machine learning, World View empowers defense, intelligence, and commercial customers to gain clarity, act with speed, and operate with confidence in complex environments

Job Description

At World View, the Warehouse Technician is responsible for supporting the maintenance of inventory in the warehouse, receiving and shipping, and kitting for the Production team. This person is regularly required to sit or stand for extended periods of time. The environment is fast paced and demanding most of the time. No travel requirements for this position.

Role Responsibilities

· Maintains an accurate inventory at all times, including count, bin location, labeling, etc.
· Receives shipments. Verifies packing slips to materials delivered, unpacks, stocks, and transfers products to the correct department.
· Maintains proper documentation of inventory and records.
· Assists in packing and labeling shipments to ensure they leave the facility within time constraints.
· Works with Quality Assurance to ensure that incoming products meet specifications.
· Works with Purchasing to communicate shipment discrepancies to vendors and packages Return to Vendor (RTV or RMA) with all needed documentation.
· Maintains min/maxes to determine fulfillment needs.
· Pulls work orders for kitting as needed to support the flight manifest and production demand.
· Monthly cycle counting and yearly physical inventory audit.
· Maintains a tidy and clean warehouse.
· Proactively tracks, reports and provides updates to management on critical issues and risks
· Proactively collaborate, as needed, with other functions, including Procurement, Finance, Quality, Manufacturing, and Engineering
· Other tasks and duties as assigned.

Qualifications

· Must have a high school diploma or GED
· Proficiency with MS Excel spreadsheets
· Proven experience using ERP systems
· Basic arithmetic skills
· A keen attention to detail
· Ability to read engineering drawings and work instructions
· Ability to solve routine problems independently
· May require lifting up to 75lbs
· Flexible to changes in business activities and priorities, able to adapt quickly and redirect efforts/resources
· Ability to establish and maintain effective, positive work relationships

Bonus Points

· Forklift driver certified

What We Offer

You will join a global, supportive, and growing team with direct exposure to a passionate and innovative CEO and our most critical decisions. With a broad scope and real influence, you will have a front row seat to scaling the business and shaping how it operates.

You will work side by side with experts in emerging technology, gaining exposure to cutting edge ideas and projects that are shaping the future. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including Medical, Dental, Vision, and Life Insurance because we believe in taking care of the you as a person, not just a team member.

Our modern office is designed to keep you inspired and energized, with a fully stocked kitchen, on-site exercise area, and stunning views that make every day brighter!

We believe in creating a dynamic and inclusive workplace where creativity thrives, and every voice is heard. Our commitment to all team members is built upon five core cultural pillars: Engage, Lean In, Create, Care, and Own. Together, these pillars form the foundation of our employee experience while adhering to the highest standards of ethical and sustainable practices.

Some Final Notes

The description gives you a sense of what the position involves but is not an exhaustive list. We are a growing and evolving team, which means your responsibilities may shift or expand over time as new opportunities arise. Flexibility and curiosity are part of what makes our work exciting!

At World View, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate belonging and diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members. We welcome applications from every background and life experience because we know our differences make us stronger.
\n

\n
$20 - $25 an hour
\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The Warehouse Technician is responsible for maintaining accurate inventory, receiving shipments, and assisting in packing and labeling shipments. They also collaborate with various departments to ensure quality and fulfillment needs are met.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a high school diploma or GED, proficiency with MS Excel, and experience using ERP systems. Attention to detail and the ability to lift up to 75lbs are also required.
linkedin_org_locations: 1805 E. Aerospace Parkway, Tucson, Arizona 85756, US
linkedin_org_description:
Since 2012, World View has led the way in remote sensing and Earth observation from stratospheric high-altitude platforms (HAPs), with demonstrated accomplishments for a variety of scientific, commercial and government organizations. Our proprietary stratospheric balloon flight technology delivers better insights with far lower costs and lead times than satellites and other traditional aerial sources. 

World View’s remotely-navigated Stratollite® flies five times closer to Earth than a satellite, capturing high-resolution imagery with much greater detail. And since it can persist over an area for weeks at a time, it provides a clearer picture of changes on the ground than a satellite that only flies over the area at the same time every day. 

Our high-resolution electro-optical, infrared, radar, hyperspectral, radio relay, and other remote sensing capabilities can deliver a wealth of insights across a range of applications. From helping agricultural yields to less-invasive energy and mineral exploration to tracking wildfires, storm systems and greenhouse gases.

title: Jr Cloud Security Engineer (R-00121)
description_text:
True Zero Technologies, a veteran-owned small business, was founded on the principle that the purposeful enablement of people and technology in an organization directly ties to the quality of its outcomes. True Zero recognizes that those outcomes begin and end with our people, and that is what we have built a community of like-minded, driven, and passionate individuals and innovators who are aligned in a common goal of delivering top-tier services to our customers. Our culture and commitment have been recognized through numerous accolades, including being named one of the Best Places to Work in 2023 in two categories (“Prosperous and Thriving” ($5MM–$50MM in gross revenue) and “Mid-Atlantic Region” (DC, DE, MD, NC, VA, WV)), and again in 2025 as a Best Places to Work honoree. In addition, True Zero earned coveted spots on the Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing companies in America in 2022, 2023, and 2025, a testament to our sustained growth driven by our people-first approach and unwavering dedication to excellence.
\n

Position Responsibilities 
Monitoring & Compliance: Monitor cloud environments for security threats, alerts, and anomalies.
Identity & Access Management (IAM): Manage user permissions, roles, and access controls to secure cloud resources.
Policy Implementation: Deploy and configure security protocols, firewalls, and security groups.
Vulnerability Management: Assist with vulnerability scanning, risk assessment, and remediation of cloud-hosted applications.
Automation: Help develop Infrastructure as Code (IaC) templates with a security-first approach.
Support: Assist senior engineers in incident response, forensics, and security audit

Position Qualifications 
Education: Min of 3 years experience & Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field (or equivalent experience).
Cloud Platforms: Basic knowledge of AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud platforms.
Security Knowledge: Understanding of networking, security principles (firewalls, encryption), and IAM.
Scripting: Familiarity with Python, PowerShell, or Bash for automation.
Certifications: AWS Cloud Practitioner, CompTIA Security+, or entry-level Azure/GCP certifications 

Role Summary 
Support the staff in securing cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP) by implementing security policies, monitoring for threats, managing identity/access controls, and assisting with incident response.

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves monitoring cloud environments for security threats and managing user permissions and access controls. Additionally, the engineer will assist with incident response and vulnerability management.

title: Principal Data Warehouse Architect
description_text:
At TTEC Digital, we coach clients to ensure their employees feel valued, and fully supported, because an amazing customer experience is an employee first process. Our vision is the same, a place where employees know they can thrive.

We are seeking an experienced Principal Data Warehouse Architect to join our Analytics and Insights team. Reporting to a Director, the person in this role will lead high‑impact Data Science and Engineering initiatives, focusing on the design and architecture of Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services. You will play a key role in transforming transactional raw data into structured, actionable insights that drive decision‑making and business growth.
\n

What you'll be doing:
Design and deploy scalable Google Cloud services in GCP.
Implement IAM access controls to ensure secure and compliant data environments.
Develop and implement robust data ingestion pipelines from diverse sources.
Develop and enforce data validation processes to maintain accuracy and reliability.
Enhance data quality and efficiency through continuous optimization.
Analyze raw data to uncover patterns, trends, and opportunities.
Produce clear design documentation and solution roadmaps.
Lead projects independently while collaborating effectively within larger teams.
Mentor and cross‑train junior and senior Data Engineers, sharing expertise on complex assignments.
Advance your skills through hands‑on experience and formal learning opportunities.
Support pre‑sales activities by providing accurate work estimates and technical input.
Partner closely with Project Management to deliver projects on time and within budget.
Travel occasionally as required for project or team needs.

Key Competencies you'll bring:
The Principal Architect demonstrates exceptional interpersonal skills, building trusted relationships through integrity, honesty, and clear communication. With high energy, adaptability, and resilience, they thrive in dynamic environments, prioritizing effectively and solving complex problems with resourcefulness.

As a leader, they inspire confidence and motivate diverse teams with a positive, forward‑looking attitude. They provide mentorship and guidance to junior colleagues, particularly on sensitive or high‑stakes projects, while championing inclusivity and collaboration to drive collective success.

Operationally, they oversee multiple projects and products with precision and efficiency, delivering customer‑centric solutions while optimizing time and resources. Their deep expertise enables them to resolve complex technical and service challenges, ensuring operational excellence through disciplined execution and continuous improvement.

Technically, they translate complex concepts into clear, actionable insights for varied audiences. Proficient with Microsoft Project, Visio, and Office tools, they leverage architectural expertise to design scalable, resilient data solutions while staying current with emerging technologies to drive innovation in data engineering practices.


Technical skills we're looking for:
Proficiency in Terraform for infrastructure automation.
Advanced programming skills in Python (pandas, numpy, PySpark).
Strong SQL expertise across MS SQL, OracleDB, and Teradata.
Hands‑on experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services.
Familiarity with CI/CD tools to streamline deployment.
Expertise in Big Data pipeline tools (Spark, Pig, Hive, Sqoop, Kafka).
Knowledge of Data Governance tools such as DLP and Dataplex.
Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments.
Skilled in REST services and API management, including:
Implementing API proxies via Apigee X/Apigee Edge.
Designing, developing, and testing APIs with Swagger/OpenAPI specifications.

Education, Skills, Experience and Certifications we're looking for:
Post‑secondary degree or diploma in Computer Science, MIS, or IT‑related field (BA/BS in unrelated field considered with relevant experience).
8+ years of experience in Data Engineering.
3+ years of application design and development in a cloud environment.
2+ years building and deploying containerized applications in Kubernetes.
2+ years coding REST services and APIs using Python, C#, Node.js, or Java.
Google Cloud Certified Professional Data Engineer preferred.
Google Cloud Certified Cloud DevOps Engineer preferred (optional).

\n
#LI-BN1

About Us
TTEC Digital and our 1,800+ employees, pioneer engagement and growth solutions that fuel the exceptional customer experience (CX). Our sister company, TTEC Engage, is a 60,000+ employee service company, with customer service representatives located around the world. TTEC Holdings Inc. is the parent company for both Digital and Engage. When clients have a holistic need, they can draw from these independently managed centers of excellence, TTEC Digital and TTEC Engage.

We are also delighted to share that TTEC has been awarded the Great Place To Work 2024-2025 certification based on outstanding employee experience across 14 countries.

TTEC is a proud equal opportunity employer where all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability. TTEC has fully embraced and is committed to expanding our diverse and inclusive workforce. We strive to reflect the communities we serve while delivering amazing service and technology centered around humanity.

Rarely do applicants meet all desired job qualifications, so if you feel you would succeed in the role above, please take a moment and share your qualifications.
#LI-RemotePhilippines
